programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A childhood friend of Gibbs' dead daughter is stalked by a marine who recently returned from Iraq, and asks the agent for help. Memories of his murdered family lead him to make the case a priority, but he acts on his initiative and leaves HQ without his badge, making DiNozzo fear he is risking his job for a personal vendetta. Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly star.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A taxi driver and an admiral working at the Pentagon are found executed by the side of a road, but the agents have trouble ascertaining a motive - until they uncover links to a woman searching for her missing husband, a political refugee from Africa. Meanwhile, McGee makes a discovery about his new girlfriend. Starring Sean Murray, Michael Weatherly and Mark Harmon.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Abby babysits a bright nine-year-old while the other members of the team try to locate the youngster's missing father - a survivalist wanted in connection with a case of robbery and murder. The search takes them deep into the wilderness - where a startling discovery awaits. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Sean Murray.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A traumatised marine escapes from a psychiatric unit, convinced he is still fighting in Iraq. The agents believe his erratic behaviour indicates post-traumatic stress disorder and try to calmly talk him round before restraining him. However, blood tests reveal high levels of steroids in his system - meaning someone is using him as a human lab rat. Starring Lauren Holly, Sean Murray and Cote de Pablo.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Muslim US Marine is found seemingly shot dead near a mosque that has been linked to an al-Qaeda recruiter. Ducky refuses to perform an autopsy out of respect for the man's religion, but Abby runs tests that reveal his demise was actually due to poisoning from a rare octopus venom. Meanwhile, McGee goes into the mosque intending to plant a concealed microphone - but discovers someone has had the same idea.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A special agent forms a team of geniuses to combat hi-tech threats of the modern age, and hires former child prodigy Walter O'Brien to lead the gang. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The governor's daughter is 'bio-hacked' and given a deadly virus. She only has 24 hours to live, so Scorpion is called in to save her. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates when a mysterious, fast-spreading illness strikes children from military families. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A petty officer is killed by a stolen surveillance drone and the team links it to elusive terrorist Benham Parsa. NCIS joins forces with the Department of Defence to track down the device before it is used for a large-scale attack, but Gibbs is less than pleased that he has to work with his ex-lover Hollis Mann. Meanwhile, McGee hopes he will be granted time off to attend a black-tie gala honouring his girlfriend Delilah. Guest starring Susanna Thompson (Arrow).(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: In an effort to find the terrorist Parsa, Gibbs and the NCIS team track down one of his cohorts using evidence gleaned from the drone attack which has killed six people and wounded a further 22. Meanwhile, an emotionally shaken McGee struggles with the ramifications of the incident. Drama, starring Mark Harmon, Sean Murray, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The murder of a port authority officer reveals Parsa's possible whereabouts as the team continues the hunt for the terrorist intent on wiping out NCIS. Mark Harmon and Emily Wickersham star.(n)

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Norwich City v West Ham United at Carrow Road and Sheffield United v Chelsea at Bramall Lane. The clash between Norwich and West Ham could prove crucial in the battle to avoid the drop, while Chelsea were looking to further enhance their chances of playing Champions League football next term with victory over the Blades. Plus, Watford v Newcastle United, Liverpool v Burnley and Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester City.(n)

programme.title: Alan Hansen: Player and Pundit
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents a look at the respective careers of the ex-Liverpool star, who is soon to retire from his role at the BBC, which has seen him serve as an expert analyst since 1992. As a player he represented Liverpool in over 400 appearances between 1977 and 1991, during a glittering era that saw the club win eight league titles and three European Cups, among other honours. Hansen was also a member of the team during the infamous disasters at Hillsborough in 1989 and the Heysel Stadium in Belgium during the 1985 European Cup final, both of which he talks about candidly, revealing the impact they have had on him personally. After retiring as a player he went on to become one of the more respected TV analysts in the game, and was known for his passionate, forthright and intelligent views that led to him being referred to as the 'King of the Pundits' by his colleague Lineker, who is joined by the likes of Kenny Dalglish, Graeme Souness, Des Lynam, Gary Neville and Mark Lawrenson in.(n)

programme.title: X+Y (2014)
programme.desc: A specially awkward teenager finds it difficult to communicate with people, but finds comfort in his passion for numbers. However, while training to take part in the forthcoming International Mathematical Olympiad, he finds love with a kindred spirit. Comedy drama, starring Asa Butterfield, Rafe Spall and Sally Hawkins.(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.desc: Aled Jones is at Leicester's De Montfort Hall to count down the UK's 10 favourite hymns as voted for by viewers, with performances of the chosen tunes by the Kingdom Choir, Lesley Garrett, Colin Thackery, Russell Watson, Sope Aluko, Eddi Reader, Katherine Jenkins and Daniel O' Donnell.(n)

programme.title: Wimbledon: The Greatest Championships
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents as a panel of experts select the best 10 men's finals of the Open era, featuring this generation's big four of Andy Murray, Roger Federer, Novak Djokovic and Rafael Nadal, as well as greats of the past such as Bjorn Borg, Boris Becker, John McEnroe and Arthur Ashe.(n)

programme.title: Billie Jean King: Born to Lead
programme.desc: As a tennis player, Billie Jean King won 39 Grand Slam titles, including 12 in singles, and is regarded by many in the sport as one of the greatest women's players of all time. In 1973 she famously defeated Bobby Riggs in a match dubbed the Battle of Sexes, which played a significant role in developing greater respect and recognition for women athletes. In this programme, she talks to Sue Barker about her sexuality and life as an activist and advocate for gender equality, LGBT rights and social justice.(n)

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: A portrait of the Kanneh-Masons, exploring the music-making of Stuart and Kadiatu's seven gifted children as well as family life at home in lockdown, culminating in a concert.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani and Matt Baker visit the Blean, an ancient woodland in the heart of Kent where they get an exclusive look at a unique wilding project. There is a report from Gloucestershire on the elusive pine marten, while one of the driest springs on record is causing big problems at Adam Henson's farm.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Piece Hall in Halifax, the only surviving 18th-century cloth hall where textiles were once traded. Items featured include a sapphire that jewellery specialist John Benjamin thinks is the biggest he has ever seen - but which the owner thinks is fake. Other finds include a Chinese imperial robe, a mysterious set of 17th-century doors, and the oldest piece of glass to ever appear on the show.(n)

programme.title: The Luminaries
programme.desc: Anna is uninjured and can't explain why, while one of the Godspeed's passengers discovers Emery trapped in a crate below deck and is terrified when he witnesses the man suddenly, inexplicably, receiving a bullet to the chest. The men of Hokitika begin to piece together what happened on the night Crosbie Wells died, while Emery and Anna reconcile and, at their first kiss, a cosmic transference occurs and they trade souls. That night, they are visited by the ghost of Crosbie Wells.(n)

programme.desc: Gabby Logan reviews the day's Premier League action, including Tottenham Hotspur v Arsenal at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium and Bournemouth v Leicester City at Vitality Stadium. Spurs and the Gunners shared the spoils in a 2-2 draw at Emirates Stadium back in September, but both teams will have been eager to take all three points on this occasion. Meanwhile, Bournemouth were in need of points in their battle against relegation, while Leicester were looking to boost their changes of finishing in the top four. Plus, Aston Villa v Crystal Palace, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Everton and a round-up of yesterday's goals.(n)

programme.title: Looking for Alaska

programme.desc: Julia Somerville, Gloria Hunniford and Angela Rippon investigate a supermarket loyalty card scheme that refuses to refund customers who have had their points stolen by scammers.(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell looks round a Birmingham semi-detached and Martin Roberts follows the progress of a warehouse building in Liverpool and a house in the West Midlands town of Stafford.(n)

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Anna has to decide about her future with Ryan, while Claudia and Pav may be on the verge of parting company. Australian drama, starring Fiona Press and Mitchell Bourke.(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is on a property-searching mission in the Devon countryside, helping a mother and daughter duo with £750,000 find a home for three generations of their family and an eco-camping business. The presenter also visits Chagford Community Farm, one of the UK's first community-supported micro-dairies and sees how residents are readily signing up to buying its local produce.(n)

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: A molten glass specialist, an Icelandic abstract artist and a self-taught metal sculptor create artworks for lottery winners who want to celebrate the day their lives changed.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades investigates the UK's espionage history through three items restored on the programme, including a bike and teapot linked to undercover operatives.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Scott and Gethin Jones presents the first visit of the week to the studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: While the rest of the UK has struggled to contain rising levels of knife and gun crime, Scotland has dramatically reduced violent crime in the past 15 years. But how was it done? Kate Silverton films with Police Scotland's Violence Reduction Unit, which tries to prevent crime by offering more help and compassion to those at risk of offending.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Max Bowden and Tony Clay to talk all things Ben Mitchell and Callum Highway - better known to fans as Ballum - with the pair reflecting on classic scenes and sharing their best backstage stories. The programme also delves into the EastEnders archives for a look at the pioneering LGBTQ+ characters that paved the way, from Colin and Barry to Binnie and Della and Christian and Syed.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: James Acaster, Dani Dyer, Oti Mabuse and Fred Sirieix join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies.(n)

programme.title: The Secrets She Keeps
programme.desc: The Australian drama continues by delving into Agatha's childhood within the tight-knit, conservative Jehovah's Witness community, and a terrible tragedy that has left her emotionally scarred is revealed. Meanwhile, Hayden, who on special leave from the Navy, is becoming desperate when Agatha refuses to take his calls, and Meghan is in hospital preparing for the birth of her baby , unaware that she might be in danger. Adaptation of Michael Robotham's novel, starring Laura Carmichael and Jessica De Gouw.(n)

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: An actor who was initially involved in the play, but dropped out due to scheduling conflicts, comes back to haunt the production. Meanwhile, Georgia tells Anna that she is due to be a birthing partner for her friend, and Michael is blackmailed by one of his neighbours. Comedy, starring David Tennant and Michael Sheen.(n)

programme.title: I May Destroy You
programme.desc: Arabella's obsession with fellow young author Della leads to unexpected progress on her book, while she continues staking out the scene of her attack. Kwame wades into uncharted waters with Tyrone and attempts to make amends with a disgruntled ex-flame, while Terry has some luck in both work and romance.(n)

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: Jade Hassoune hosts as the contestants divide into teams to act their way into Canada's drag her-story, with judges Brooke Lynn Hytes, Jeffrey Bowyer-Chapman and Stacey McKenzie.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: A fantasy-inspired wedding full of magical treats - especially for wedding planner and Wolfblood fan Amy who discovers star Leona will be helping her plan the even. Mum and Dad have to retrieve their rings from a tree stump infested with creepy crawlies and journey into an enchanted forest on unicorns before their fantasy wedding can magically appear.(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood investigate gruesome goings on, including a strange slime invading people's gardens, and a plant that pretends to be a dead animal.(n)

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and friends explore a folk park in Omagh.(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: The presenters celebrate the Purple Badge, with help from the Dengineers and gymnast Nile Wilson. Plus, the cast of Malory Towers join Mwaksy Mudenda to bake some tasty treats.(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve heads to Norway to dive with the deadliest predator of all: the orca.(n)

programme.title: Steve Backshall's Extreme Mountain Challenge
programme.desc: Part two of two. The naturalist reaches the peak of one of Venezuela's tepui mountains, before joining a party of Italian cavers as they venture into the unexplored caverns within. Inside, they discover strange wildlife and evidence of the geological forces that give the tepuis their unusual shape. Finally, the team returns to the ground by abseiling down the Angel Falls, the world's highest waterfall.(n)

programme.title: Big Cats
programme.desc: The second documentary looks at how cats have adapted to the challenges of life in almost every environment on Earth. The margay leaps through the treetops in the Central American jungle, while the pregnant jaguar waits for thousands of turtles to arrive on a remote Pacific beach. In the Karoo region of South Africa, a tiny black-footed cat stakes its claim to the title of the world's deadliest feline, and in Outer Mongolia, the Pallas's cat is able to disguise itself as a rock - a useful trick while hunting.(n)

programme.title: Wild West: America's Great Frontier
programme.desc: Wildlife in America's mountain regions, including the Rockies and the Sierra Nevada, areas infamous for extremes of temperature from freezing winters to scorching summers. The programme examines the survival strategies employed by animals including black bears, pikas and bison, as well as parasitic plants. Plus, a look at the challenges faced by these region's human inhabitants, including ranchers leading their cattle on 70-mile journeys.(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain heads to the West Country, picking fruit with jam maker Sarah Churchill and creating a mixed berry conserve to use in doughnuts. She also visits garlic farmer Mark Botwright and is put to work digging garlic bulbs, which inspires her recipe for apple walnut and coriander salad with burnt garlic dressing. Nadiya also puts her twist on local classics, with a tropical cream tea, and lamb and apple pasties.(n)

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Brian Turner accompanies astrologer Russell Grant as he visits places from his childhood, including the church where he was a choirboy and the flat where he lived with his parents.(n)

programme.title: The 39 Steps (1935)
programme.desc: A Canadian in London offers a safe hiding place to a British secret agent investigating a spy ring. However, when she is murdered, he realises he will be the prime suspect and goes on the run with the intention of exposing the real killers. Alfred Hitchcock's thriller based on John Buchan's novel, starring Robert Donat, Madeleine Carroll, Lucie Mannheim, Godfrey Tearle and Peggy Ashcroft.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Exploring the relationship between director Alfred Hitchcock and his stars, featuring interviews with the man himself, Kim Novak, Tippi Hedren, Joan Fontaine, Janet Leigh and Sean Connery. Sylvia Syms narrates.(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: A journey around some of the most dramatic coastlines in Europe, uncovering the stories behind how these landscapes have shaped history.(n)

programme.title: Greatest Escapes to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman looks back at some of the most remote properties featured on Escape to the Country, as well as homes that offer the finest views. She also recalls rural businesses and activities that have taken presenters off the beaten track.(n)

programme.title: A Wild Year
programme.desc: Water has always been the driving force in East Anglia - its ebb and flow has shaped the fenland. The ancient wetlands once covered hundreds of square miles, but today, well over 90 per cent has been drained and converted into farmland. Every winter, thousands of whooper swans return to the flooded pastures of the Ouse washes from their Arctic breeding grounds. On the Welney Wetlands, spring sees 'mad' march hares boxing over mates. It was once thought these bouts were male hares boxing for dominance, but it is often the females throwing the punches to fend off the attentions of over-eager suitors. Narrated by Toby Jones.(n)

programme.title: Inside Central Station
programme.desc: Staff and passengers celebrate Glasgow Pride, students use the concourse as a location for a film, and signaller Brian explains how Victorian engineering keeps the network running.(n)

programme.title: A House Through Time
programme.desc: David Olusoga continues to chart British history through the successive inhabitants of a terraced house in Liverpool, focusing on the 1850s to the 1890s. David uncovers the stories of a widow who turned the dwelling into a boarding house, and an ambitious Jewish immigrant struggling to stay afloat in the midst of a financial crisis. He also traces the story of a seemingly successful couple for whom that house was the backdrop to a family drama involving violence and infidelity and reveals how an unidentified body dragged from the Mersey turned out to have surprising connections to the house.(n)

programme.title: Test Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents action from the fourth day of the First Test in the three-match series, held at Ageas Bowl in Southampton. With analysis from Michael Vaughan and commentary by Alastair Cook, Carlos Brathwaite, Phil Tufnell and Alison Mitchell.(n)

programme.title: British History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Historian Lucy Worsley debunks the so-called Glorious Revolution of 1688, when Dutch prince William of Orange invaded the British Isles and stole the throne from his father-in-law, King James II. It has since been described as a 'glorious' and 'bloodless' revolution. But given that it led to huge slaughter in Ireland and Scotland, how glorious was it really?(n)

programme.title: Live Aid
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the 1985 benefit concert, when stars including Elton John, Paul McCartney, Queen, David Bowie, U2 and a host of others walked out to a crowd of 72,000 at Wembley Stadium - and a global TV audience of billions - to raise money for the famine in Ethiopia. Bob Geldof and others talk about their fears at not being able to pull it off after 12 weeks of preparation, while famous music fans Tony Blair, JK Rowling and Helen Fielding remember watching it at home.(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Grace Jones presents a look back at a year when Live Aid took the world by storm, Prince and Bruce Springsteen topped the charts, and Miami Vice set the standard in men's fashion. Anarchic comic Viz hit newsagents' shelves nationwide and the second series of Spitting Image poked fun at the likes of Roy Hattersley and David Steel, who discuss how it felt to be on the receiving end.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Memorable hits of 1985, featuring TOTP performances spanning the genres. Performers include Wham!, the Smiths, Kate Bush, A-ha, Eurythmics, the Cure, Dead or Alive, Bonnie Tyler, Tears for Fears, Sister Sledge, Jennifer Rush and Doug E Fresh.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: 1985 sees the 'Big Four' of 80's Pop - Spandau, Wham, Culture Club and the Durans - have a relatively quiet year which ushers in a new generation of pop talent. The show becomes a barometer for the state of the nation as the ambitious King and A-ha, propelled by the most expensive video of the year for Take On Me, compete for attention with passionate politicos Billy Bragg and The Style Council who, fired up by the ongoing miners' strike and continuing threat of nuclear war, give the studio audience their all with Between The Wars and Walls Come Tumbling Down. 1985 also sees electro dance hit the mainstream as Dead or Alive top the charts with You Spin Me Round, the first number one for producers Stock, Aitken and Waterman, and Paul Hardcastle holds the top slot for five weeks with 19, a spoken word single made with new sampling technology and released on the 10th anniversary of the end of the Vietnam war. Meanwhile, movie tie-ins and record companies eyeing a 'housewives' market see a.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: The Edible Garden
programme.desc: Alys Fowler demonstrates how easy it is to grow raspberries and blueberries, and plants juicy fruits from which she then prepares jams and tangy fruit leathers. She also tries her hand at hapazome, a Japanese printing technique used to transfer flower and leaf shapes on to fabric.(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Presenter Helen Skelton and expert garden designer Danny Clarke head to Bradford to breathe new life into a thoroughly overgrown garden lost in time. The aim is that they will get the job completed in just one day for owner Beverley and her family.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker explores the stretch of the Grand Union Canal that runs close to his home in the Chilterns - meeting those who live and work on the waterway and also getting to grips with an engineering marvel that keeps the whole system flowing. Elsewhere, Tom Heap investigates how farmers are adjusting to the challenges posed by climate change, and John Craven launches this year's Countryfile photography competition with the help of singer and broadcaster Cerys Matthews and wildlife expert Simon King.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don checks the progress of the potatoes he planted earlier in the year and recommends plants for dry shade under trees. There are water-saving tips from a water scientist in Somerset who uses a variety of techniques to save as much water as possible in her own garden and advice from a head gardener in Dorset, dedicated to growing plants for their aromatic properties both in leaf and flower. Plus, Nick Bailey on summer-flowering clematis and Ant Anderson meeting people planting up the Birmingham canal sides.(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter demonstrates the ultimate in no-fuss, easy-to-grow strawberries and Chris Beardshaw shows how to prune figs and grapes for summer.(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Spain
programme.desc: The chef continues his culinary tour of Spain by travelling east from the Basque region toward the Mediterranean coast, sampling a selection of great dishes and enjoying local hospitality along the way.(n)

programme.title: The Good Cook
programme.desc: Food writer Simon Hopkinson demonstrates how to create restaurant-quality food at home, preparing risotto alla Parmigiana, tandoori chicken, and Parma ham and asparagus pancakes. He also creates a quiche Lorraine and a coffee granita.(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Following the life of a sea otter pup growing up on the coast of California, in an unusual location among the yachts of a marina. Its mother must teach her baby how to avoid the boats and find food in the busy harbour, but an attack by a tough male otter signals disaster for the family. Narrated by Bonnie Greer.(n)

programme.title: The Choir: Singing for Britain
programme.desc: Gareth Malone sets out to bring the nation together through music during the Coronavirus lockdown, by getting people from all walks of life to perform a specially composed song. Gareth employs technology to bring the voices of performers from across the country together online even if social distancing makes large gatherings impossible. The final episode features the country's most vulnerable people, who have contributed to the project while self-shielding, and discovers their personal stories of finding hope during isolation.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: The life of acting legend Michael Caine, star of movies such as The Ipcress File (being shown next), The Italian Job, and The Dark Knight. Using rare TV interviews and classic archive clips, this programme tells the story of one of Britain's most successful actors. Narrated by Sylvia Syms.(n)

programme.title: The Ipcress File (1965)
programme.desc: An intelligence agent investigating the kidnap and brainwashing of Britain's most renowned scientists unpicks a complex chain of connections and high-level intrigue, while detecting evidence of double-dealing and counter-espionage. Spy thriller based on Len Deighton's novel, starring Michael Caine, Sue Lloyd, Gordon Jackson and Nigel Green.(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: At Anstruther station in Scotland, one of their newest helms, George, takes charge for his first shout to a serious medical evacuation. A woman has slipped on one of the most notorious parts of the station's patch - the Chain Walk. The RNLI volunteers also race to the rescue of two teenagers trapped in a cave, and when Storm Henry hits the UK, two lifeboats must battle to save a cargo ship from disaster.(n)

programme.title: This World
programme.desc: Two years after Cuba announced radical economic reforms, broadcaster Simon Reeve heads to the Caribbean island to look at the effects the liberalisation has had. He meets Cubans whose lives are being transformed - from the owners of fledgling businesses to the newly rich estate agents selling properties valued at up to £750,000 - and asks if this new openness could lead to more political freedom. He also explores whether Cuba will be able to maintain the positive aspects of its long isolation under socialism - such as low crime, high-quality education and one of the best healthcare systems in the world - while embracing a form of capitalism. Part of the This World strand.(n)

programme.title: Test Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents action from the fifth and final day of the First Test in the three-match series, held at Ageas Bowl in Southampton. With analysis from Michael Vaughan and commentary by Alastair Cook, Carlos Brathwaite, Phil Tufnell and Alison Mitchell.(n)

programme.title: One Day: Sport's Super Sunday
programme.desc: A look back at a memorable sporting day on 14 July last year, when England looked to win the ICC Cricket World Cup for the first time as they took on New Zealand in the final at Lord's, while Roger Federer and Novak Djokovic contested a high-quality Wimbledon final at the All England Club.(n)

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan: From My Sofa
programme.desc: The comedian revisits memorable moments from his travel series, beginning with a look back at the episodes where he explored Zimbabwe, Bosnia and the Sahara. The programme features some never-before-seen footage shot for these episodes, including the moment when Romesh tried to make a horror film in the desert.(n)

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Extreme Love
programme.desc: In the first of two documentaries focusing on neurological conditions, Louis visits a school in America for people with autism - a developmental disorder characterised by difficulties with social interaction and communication. The DLC Warren in New Jersey is one of the most innovative centres of its kind, with specialist intervention designed to help both the students and the families who care for them. He meets 13-year-old Joey, whose mother Carol is finding it increasingly difficult to cope with his explosive tantrums, 19-year-old Nicky, who has written a novel, and 20-year-old Brian, whose extreme behaviour has led to the decision to place him in residential care.(n)

programme.title: Mum's List (2016)
programme.desc: Young couple Kate and Singe's lives are turned upside down when Kate is diagnosed with an incurable breast cancer. Over the course of her final few months, she creates a list - writing her thoughts and memories down to help the man she loved create the best life possible for their two sons after she is gone. Drama based on the book by St John Greene, starring Rafe Spall, Emilia Fox and Elaine Cassidy.(n)

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: A busy night shift begins with Chelsey and Dave providing back-up for advanced paramedic Rob at a road traffic accident, where a driver has overturned his car at a roundabout. It is clear he has been drinking, meaning once they have assessed him, he will be answering questions from the police. Eric and Emily are dispatched to an 83-year-old who is in a lot of pain from her back, after which they answer a call from the police to a suspected assault on a 12-year-old, while senior paramedic Tony is called out to a man found slumped in his car in the city centre.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Nicky is stressed and exhausted, with devastating consequences at work. As she struggles to cope with the pressure from her mother, a tragic incident pushes her over the edge. Ange tells Cameron he is not ready to take the next step in his career. Frustrated by the rejection, he takes out his anger on Louis. Sacha decides to respect Essie's wishes concerning her cancer treatment, but Dominic urges him to persuade her not to give up on palliative care.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: The three artists travel to a home in Birmingham, hoping to assist a buyer born on the weekend of the moon landings, who wants to mark her 50th birthday year with something out of this world.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades recalls three barn fixes - a butter churn, a spinning wheel and a set of ploughing trophies - to trace the changing face of rural life in the UK.(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet Revisited
programme.desc: In the first of two programmes, Steve Backshall discovers why the Bahamas provide a sanctuary for sharks, looking at the protection offered to them, people's changing attitudes and the key role science plays in their conservation. He joins researchers who have dedicated their lives to this unique and often misunderstood group of animals and learns that a healthy shark population is absolutely vital to a healthy marine ecosystem.(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament.(n)

programme.title: Into the Wild with Gordon Buchanan
programme.desc: The film-maker takes Dermot O'Leary to the Western Isles, where they search for seals and golden eagles on Skye, then canoe out to a shag colony so the X Factor presenter can get close to seabirds. At breakfast, an encounter with a magnificent stag gets them off to a good start, but what Dermot is really keen to see are basking sharks. Can Gordon possibly succeed in finding one?(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Susan Calman hosts the quiz in which one contestant is put in control and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which of the other players is best suited to answer.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bakers Shorts
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers bake white rolls in a Doncaster market then mill their own flour at a windmill in Lincolnshire to make a classic brown loaf for a ploughman's lunch.(n)

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode continues his alphabetical tour through the BBC's culinary archive, this time selecting recipes and ingredients linked by the letter E. Raymond Blanc demonstrates how to make chocolate eclairs, and Giorgio Locatelli and Tony Allan cook eels and mash.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at the TV appearances made over the years by Swedish Academy Award-winning actress Ingrid Bergman, exploring the milestones of her life and career, including her appearances in Casablanca and Spellbound. Narrated by Sylvia Syms.(n)

programme.title: Joan of Arc (1948)
programme.desc: Multi-Oscar-nominated but ponderously paced historical drama adapted from Maxwell Anderson's stage play, charting the last battle undertaken by the medieval heroine of Orleans. Starring Ingrid Bergman, Jose Ferrer and George Coulouris.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: David Harper and Raj Bisram value items brought into Aberystwyth University's Old College, and Paul Martin finds out how the myth of Merlin has been connected to the heart of Wales.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Part one of two. Michael Portillo ventures Down Under to explore Australia by train. He begins by riding the legendary Ghan Railway, a train almost a kilometre in length and hauled by two locomotives, which covers nearly 2,000 miles across Australia's red centre. Michael traces its route from the southern harbour of Port Augusta to Darwin on the north coast, along the way meeting the indigenous people of Alice Springs.(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Ellis family from Bradford discover how life has changed for ordinary working families in the north of England over the last 100 years. Jon, Lesley, Caitlin, Freya and Harvey experience the lives of previous generations, from the food people ate to the jobs they did and how they enjoyed themselves. It opens just after the end of the First World War, and the north is on the cusp of great transformation. Presenter Sara Cox and social historian Polly Russell introduce the family to 1918 living, and Anita Rani introduces them to the pleasures of rambling, 1930s-style.(n)

programme.title: Our Friend Victoria
programme.desc: The comedy of Victoria Wood is celebrated by her friend and collaborator Julie Walters, who looks at the characters her friend created that spanned generations. Beloved clips include Chrissie, the long-distance swimmer, dinnerladies, Acorn Antiques, Waitress and 14 Again. There are also contributions from colleagues including Celia Imrie, Duncan Preston, Anne Reid, Maxine Peake, Thelma Barlow and Shobna Gulati.(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman presides as the academic quiz returns for its 27th series since the revival of the show in 1995, with the opening match of the first round seeing four students from the University of Glasgow take on a quartet from the University of Exeter.(n)

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Iraq
programme.desc: Documentary examining the realities of war and life under Isis, with unique personal archive from civilians and soldiers from both sides of the conflict. As war began, many Iraqis were hopeful. Ahmed Al Bashir, now a renowned comedian, remembers practising his English with US troops before the realities of occupation became clear. Waleed Nesyif was 18 when George Bush gave Saddam Hussein just 48 hours to leave Iraq. He was, like many Iraqi teenagers at that time, infatuated by the West. But while many of his generation grew up enjoying songs by the Backstreet Boys, Waleed formed Iraq's first heavy metal band. Narrated by Andy Serkis.(n)

programme.title: Comedians: Home Alone
programme.desc: Gavin & Stacey star Mathew Horne reveals how life in isolation is familiar territory for an actor, Tim Key provides food for thought from his kitchen and Rachel Parris and Marcus Brigstocke perform another showstopping lip sync battle.(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Topical comedy quiz show, hosted by Dara O Briain. Regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on another eventful week in news and politics, with the help of fellow comedians Angela Barnes, Ed Byrne, Rhys James, Nish Kumar and Suzi Ruffell. Stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on each other.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Live Aid
programme.desc: Two-part documentary recounting the story behind the fund-raising concert. The first half deals with the lead-up to the event, when it seemed everything might end in disaster. With the performance date mere weeks away, Bob Geldof and his colleagues found themselves with no acts, no venue and no broadcaster. Geldof, Midge Ure and promoter Harvey Goldsmith discuss how they persuaded the stars to come on board, while Elton John, David Bowie, Paul McCartney, and members of U2 and Queen reminisce about the big day itself.(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: On the Isle of Lewis in the Outer Hebrides, crofter Sandy Granville assesses the damage to his croft after one of the worst storms on record. Meanwhile, farmers in the north of Aberdeen and near Inverness scan their pregnant ewes, and on the West Coast, two hill farmers try to return four male tup lambs to a close friend. Plus, John Scott and Martine Irvine head to the Stirling Bull Sales in the hope of selling their best bulls.(n)

programme.title: Earth's Natural Wonders
programme.desc: Documentary exploring some of the most inhospitable places on the planet concludes by shedding light on the lives of the Kamayura People, who reside in Brazil's Xingu Indigenous Park. For the Kamayura, pleasing the spirits is crucial to maintaining good health, and during one key festival, a warrior must dance for hours to appease a bird spirit, sustained only by food and drink brought to him by villagers. In Ethiopia, belief drives the residents of the Tigray region to climb a 400-metre mountain to reach their church. In Switzerland, the Zermatt air rescue team offers an insight into why millions of hikers, climbers and skiers visit the Swiss Alps every year, despite the risk of injury - or even death.(n)

programme.title: The Young Montalbano
programme.desc: The town of Vigata is shaken by the murder of a rich old man, and Montalbano must overcome a lack of concrete evidence as he investigates a mystery killer thought to be behind a series of animal shootings. In Italian.(n)

programme.title: Sicily: Wonder of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Michael Scott concludes his history of Sicily, telling the story of how the Christian Byzantine Empire was swept aside by a Muslim Arab invasion from North Africa. The island was transformed by new trade networks and agricultural techniques, and in the 19th century was unified with Italy - but the greatest threat to their prosperity then came from within and the rise of the Mafia.(n)

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Part two of two. Michael Portillo heads to Gargnano to visit the romantic hideaway of DH Lawrence before ending his journey with a high-speed boat trip across Lake Garda.(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: On the Isle of Lewis in the Outer Hebrides, crofter Sandy Granville assesses the damage to his croft after one of the worst storms on record. Meanwhile, farmers in the north of Aberdeen and near Inverness scan their pregnant ewes, and on the West Coast, two hill farmers try to return four male tup lambs to a close friend. Plus, John Scott and Martine Irvine head to the Stirling Bull Sales in the hope of selling their best bulls.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Gary Davies presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 30 November 1989 and featuring performances by 808 State, Tina Turner, Kaoma, Tears for Fears, Kate Bush, New Kids on the Block and Gloria Estefan.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mark Goodier presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 7 December 1989 and featuring performances by Jason Donovan, Big Fun, Sydney Youngblood, Kate Bush, Soul II Soul, Dusty Springfield, Jeff Wayne, Latino Rave, Simple Minds and Queen.(n)

programme.title: Earth's Natural Wonders
programme.desc: Documentary exploring some of the most inhospitable places on the planet concludes by shedding light on the lives of the Kamayura People, who reside in Brazil's Xingu Indigenous Park. For the Kamayura, pleasing the spirits is crucial to maintaining good health, and during one key festival, a warrior must dance for hours to appease a bird spirit, sustained only by food and drink brought to him by villagers. In Ethiopia, belief drives the residents of the Tigray region to climb a 400-metre mountain to reach their church. In Switzerland, the Zermatt air rescue team offers an insight into why millions of hikers, climbers and skiers visit the Swiss Alps every year, despite the risk of injury - or even death.(n)

programme.title: All Aboard! The Canal Trip
programme.desc: Cameras capture a journey down one of Britain's most historic waterways, the Kennet and Avon Canal, filmed in real time to allow viewers to take in the images and sounds of the British countryside, spot wildlife and glimpse life on the towpath as if they were there. Archive stills and graphics are used to deliver salient facts about the canal and its social history.(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Chris Lintott investigates the strange dimming of Betelgeuse, while Maggie Aderin-Pocock reports on the recent discovery of the biggest and brightest supernova ever observed. Lucie Green discovers how historical drawings that pre-date the invention of the telescope are helping to predict solar activity, and Peter Lawrence attempts to photograph our very own star.(n)

programme.title: Othello: Royal Shakespeare Company
programme.desc: Iqbal Khan's ground-breaking 2015 production, starring Hugh Quarshie as Othello, Joanna Vanderham as Desdemona and Lucian Msamati as Iago. Othello is the greatest general of his age, but he is also an outsider whose victories have created enemies of his own. As they plot in the shadows, Othello realises too late that the greatest danger lies not in the hatred of others, but his own fragile and destructive pride.(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare in Italy
programme.desc: Part one of two. The architect and historian reveals how the Mediterranean country influenced the Bard, as he takes a journey of discovery through Shakespeare's Italian-based plays. He visits the locations that inspired the playwright, beginning with Padua, setting of The Taming of the Shrew, before heading to Verona to explore the tale of Romeo and Juliet. The presenter also meets actress Emma Thompson in Sicily to discuss Much Ado About Nothing, and ends his journey in Venice for a look at the city's connection with Othello.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Heather confesses to Shirley her true feelings for Minty, Jack is surprised by Roxy's sincerity over Sean, and Christian attempts to make Steven face up to his sexuality.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Heather's happy day is thrown into turmoil by Hazel's arrival, leaving Minty with a difficult decision to make. Ronnie is upset to see Jack is taking Tanya to the wedding.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: A last-minute guest disrupts Minty's wedding, Ronnie is distraught to discover Roxy in a drunken state at Gus's flat and Shirley finally gets to know her biopsy results.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Gus awaits his fate at the hands of Sean, Ronnie's interference prompts Roxy to take drastic steps to get back at her, and the happy couple head off on honeymoon.(n)

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Jemma Woodman goes on a culinary tour of the river Dart, meeting those who get inspiration and food from this scenic part of the South West.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross's exciting use of blue, yellow and orange hues brings vibrancy to a cabin in a woodside setting beside a clear blue lake.(n)

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: Dramatised documentary examining how Hitler and the Nazi party rose to power in Germany during the 1930s, revealing the greed, manipulation and frightening underestimation of Adolf Hitler's ambition. In 1930, Germany was a liberal democracy, with elections, parliament and the rule of law, but just four years later its leader was a dictator and its government is in the hands of murderers. Leading historians and experts take viewers inside the minds of those hellbent on gaining power and those who tried to stop them.(n)

programme.title: Being Beethoven
programme.desc: A look at how the composer's return to the town of Heiligenstadt led to one of the most extraordinary outpourings of creativity in the history of music, from the earth-shattering Eroica through to Symphony No. 7, while his repeated failed attempts to find love resulted in the famous Immortal Beloved letter found after his death.(n)

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Part one of two. Michael Portillo ventures into the Black Forest to discover the essence of Germany, discovering how Hansel and Gretel helped to unify the nation and learning how to make a cuckoo clock.(n)

programme.title: Seven Ages of Britain
programme.desc: David Dimbleby studies artefacts reflecting the shift in values during the 20th century as art ceased to be an upper-class preserve, after the First World War called traditional values into question. He examines some of the masterpieces of modern British art, including Paul Nash's The Menin Road and Francis Bacon's Crucifixion, and puts the case for the lesser-appreciated spheres of broadcasting and domestic design. He also meets contemporary artists Damien Hirst, Tracey Emin, Anish Kapoor and Gilbert & George.(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Bosnian Serb general Ratko Mladic was one of the most infamous figures of the Bosnian war of the 1990s, synonymous with the merciless siege of Sarajevo in which 15,000 people were killed or wounded, and the murder of over 7,000 Muslim men and boys in Srebrenica in 1995. This documentary was filmed over the course of five years and hears from prosecution and defence lawyers and witnesses from both sides in Mladic's historic war crimes trial.(n)

programme.title: All Over the Place: Asia
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and his CBBC mates go on an adventure to find the most unusual and amazing places in Asia. They get caught up with some traditional Keralan fishing skills in India; become copycats at a cat café in Tokyo; brush up on their calligraphy writing skills in China; flush out a collector of toilets in India and race some giant straw sandals in Japan! Ed is joined on this bonkers road trip by Inel Tomlinson, Susan Calman, Cel Spellman, Victoria Cook, Hacker and Dodge, Naomi Wilkinson and Chris Johnson - there's stuff to do in Asia that is totally ace and it turns up All Over The Place!(n)

programme.title: Absolute Genius with Dick & Dom

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. As always, the challengers are tasked with coming up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. This time, all the contestants come from the world of journalism, as Owen Jones, Paris Lees, Kay Burley, Adam Boulton, Carole Malone, Quentin Letts, Amol Rajan and Victoria Derbyshire take part.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jade prepares to meet her birth mother for the first time. She's incredibly nervous, convinced she was given away because of her deafness, but Marty tries his best to reassure her. She faces her fears and heads to a cafe to meet her mum, finally uncovering the truth behind her past in the process. Meanwhile, Charlie throws himself back into work, but it's clear there is something else on his mind, and Faith attempts a conversation with Dylan but he pushes her away, claiming he needs some distance.(n)

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. This time, rapper Tinie Tempah and comedian Katherine Ryan pop by for a chat and a game of Musical VAR, Mo Farah takes on Jessica Ennis-Hill in The Sporting Fix, Peter takes Jamie Redknapp on an extreme retirement challenge and Ellie Goulding Saves Our Summer with an exclusive performance in The Garden Sessions.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Norwich City v West Ham United at Carrow Road and Sheffield United v Chelsea at Bramall Lane. The clash between Norwich and West Ham could prove crucial in the battle to avoid the drop, while Chelsea were looking to further enhance their chances of playing Champions League football next term with victory over the Blades. Plus, Watford v Newcastle United, Liverpool v Burnley and Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester City.(n)

programme.title: Alan Hansen: Player and Pundit
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents a look at the respective careers of the ex-Liverpool star, who is soon to retire from his role at the BBC, which has seen him serve as an expert analyst since 1992. As a player he represented Liverpool in over 400 appearances between 1977 and 1991, during a glittering era that saw the club win eight league titles and three European Cups, among other honours. Hansen was also a member of the team during the infamous disasters at Hillsborough in 1989 and the Heysel Stadium in Belgium during the 1985 European Cup final, both of which he talks about candidly, revealing the impact they have had on him personally. After retiring as a player he went on to become one of the more respected TV analysts in the game, and was known for his passionate, forthright and intelligent views that led to him being referred to as the 'King of the Pundits' by his colleague Lineker, who is joined by the likes of Kenny Dalglish, Graeme Souness, Des Lynam, Gary Neville and Mark Lawrenson in.(n)

programme.title: X+Y (2014)
programme.desc: A specially awkward teenager finds it difficult to communicate with people, but finds comfort in his passion for numbers. However, while training to take part in the forthcoming International Mathematical Olympiad, he finds love with a kindred spirit. Comedy drama, starring Asa Butterfield, Rafe Spall and Sally Hawkins.(n)

programme.title: Billie Jean King: Born to Lead
programme.desc: As a tennis player, Billie Jean King won 39 Grand Slam titles, including 12 in singles, and is regarded by many in the sport as one of the greatest women's players of all time. In 1973 she famously defeated Bobby Riggs in a match dubbed the Battle of Sexes, which played a significant role in developing greater respect and recognition for women athletes. In this programme, she talks to Sue Barker about her sexuality and life as an activist and advocate for gender equality, LGBT rights and social justice.(n)

There is a report from Gloucestershire on the elusive pine marten, while one of the driest springs on record is causing big problems at Adam Henson's farm.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Piece Hall in Halifax, the only surviving 18th-century cloth hall where textiles were once traded. Items featured include a sapphire that jewellery specialist John Benjamin thinks is the biggest he has ever seen - but which the owner thinks is fake. Other finds include a Chinese imperial robe, a mysterious set of 17th-century doors, and the oldest piece of glass to ever appear on the show.(n)

programme.title: The Luminaries
programme.desc: Anna is uninjured and can't explain why, while one of the Godspeed's passengers discovers Emery trapped in a crate below deck and is terrified when he witnesses the man suddenly, inexplicably, receiving a bullet to the chest. The men of Hokitika begin to piece together what happened on the night Crosbie Wells died, while Emery and Anna reconcile and, at their first kiss, a cosmic transference occurs and they trade souls. That night, they are visited by the ghost of Crosbie Wells.(n)

programme.desc: While the rest of the UK has struggled to contain rising levels of knife and gun crime, Scotland has dramatically reduced violent crime in the past 15 years. But how was it done? Kate Silverton films with Police Scotland's Violence Reduction Unit, which tries to prevent crime by offering more help and compassion to those at risk of offending.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Max Bowden and Tony Clay to talk all things Ben Mitchell and Callum Highway - better known to fans as Ballum - with the pair reflecting on classic scenes and sharing their best backstage stories. The programme also delves into the EastEnders archives for a look at the pioneering LGBTQ+ characters that paved the way, from Colin and Barry to Binnie and Della and Christian and Syed.(n)

programme.title: Gareth Edwards' Great Welsh Adventure
programme.desc: Stress levels are running high when rugby legend Gareth and wife Maureen face their first lock on the Montgomery Canal. Later, the duo enjoy a spell on a horse-drawn narrowboat, finally experiencing the tranquillity of near silence drifting through a pristine landscape without a roaring boat engine. At a section of canal that is still impassable, they learn of plans to restore the canal back to its former glory.(n)

programme.title: The Secrets She Keeps
programme.desc: The Australian drama continues by delving into Agatha's childhood within the tight-knit, conservative Jehovah's Witness community, and a terrible tragedy that has left her emotionally scarred is revealed. Meanwhile, Hayden, who on special leave from the Navy, is becoming desperate when Agatha refuses to take his calls, and Meghan is in hospital preparing for the birth of her baby , unaware that she might be in danger. Adaptation of Michael Robotham's novel, starring Laura Carmichael and Jessica De Gouw.(n)

Plus, Watford v Newcastle United, Liverpool v Burnley and Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester City.(n)

programme.title: Alan Hansen: Player and Pundit
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents a look at the respective careers of the ex-Liverpool star, who is soon to retire from his role at the BBC, which has seen him serve as an expert analyst since 1992. As a player he represented Liverpool in over 400 appearances between 1977 and 1991, during a glittering era that saw the club win eight league titles and three European Cups, among other honours. Hansen was also a member of the team during the infamous disasters at Hillsborough in 1989 and the Heysel Stadium in Belgium during the 1985 European Cup final, both of which he talks about candidly, revealing the impact they have had on him personally. After retiring as a player he went on to become one of the more respected TV analysts in the game, and was known for his passionate, forthright and intelligent views that led to him being referred to as the 'King of the Pundits' by his colleague Lineker, who is joined by the likes of Kenny Dalglish, Graeme Souness, Des Lynam, Gary Neville and Mark Lawrenson in.(n)

programme.title: X+Y (2014)
programme.desc: A specially awkward teenager finds it difficult to communicate with people, but finds comfort in his passion for numbers. However, while training to take part in the forthcoming International Mathematical Olympiad, he finds love with a kindred spirit. Comedy drama, starring Asa Butterfield, Rafe Spall and Sally Hawkins.(n)

programme.title: Wimbledon: The Greatest Championships
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents as a panel of experts select the best 10 men's finals of the Open era, featuring this generation's big four of Andy Murray, Roger Federer, Novak Djokovic and Rafael Nadal, as well as greats of the past such as Bjorn Borg, Boris Becker, John McEnroe and Arthur Ashe.(n)

programme.title: Billie Jean King: Born to Lead
programme.desc: As a tennis player, Billie Jean King won 39 Grand Slam titles, including 12 in singles, and is regarded by many in the sport as one of the greatest women's players of all time. In 1973 she famously defeated Bobby Riggs in a match dubbed the Battle of Sexes, which played a significant role in developing greater respect and recognition for women athletes. In this programme, she talks to Sue Barker about her sexuality and life as an activist and advocate for gender equality, LGBT rights and social justice.(n)

programme.desc: A portrait of the Kanneh-Masons, exploring the music-making of Stuart and Kadiatu's seven gifted children as well as family life at home in lockdown, culminating in a concert.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani and Matt Baker visit the Blean, an ancient woodland in the heart of Kent where they get an exclusive look at a unique wilding project. There is a report from Gloucestershire on the elusive pine marten, while one of the driest springs on record is causing big problems at Adam Henson's farm.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Piece Hall in Halifax, the only surviving 18th-century cloth hall where textiles were once traded. Items featured include a sapphire that jewellery specialist John Benjamin thinks is the biggest he has ever seen - but which the owner thinks is fake. Other finds include a Chinese imperial robe, a mysterious set of 17th-century doors, and the oldest piece of glass to ever appear on the show.(n)

programme.title: The Luminaries
programme.desc: Anna is uninjured and can't explain why, while one of the Godspeed's passengers discovers Emery trapped in a crate below deck and is terrified when he witnesses the man suddenly, inexplicably, receiving a bullet to the chest. The men of Hokitika begin to piece together what happened on the night Crosbie Wells died, while Emery and Anna reconcile and, at their first kiss, a cosmic transference occurs and they trade souls. That night, they are visited by the ghost of Crosbie Wells.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: While the rest of the UK has struggled to contain rising levels of knife and gun crime, Scotland has dramatically reduced violent crime in the past 15 years. But how was it done? Kate Silverton films with Police Scotland's Violence Reduction Unit, which tries to prevent crime by offering more help and compassion to those at risk of offending.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Max Bowden and Tony Clay to talk all things Ben Mitchell and Callum Highway - better known to fans as Ballum - with the pair reflecting on classic scenes and sharing their best backstage stories. The programme also delves into the EastEnders archives for a look at the pioneering LGBTQ+ characters that paved the way, from Colin and Barry to Binnie and Della and Christian and Syed.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: James Acaster, Dani Dyer, Oti Mabuse and Fred Sirieix join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies.(n)

programme.title: The Secrets She Keeps
programme.desc: The Australian drama continues by delving into Agatha's childhood within the tight-knit, conservative Jehovah's Witness community, and a terrible tragedy that has left her emotionally scarred is revealed. Meanwhile, Hayden, who on special leave from the Navy, is becoming desperate when Agatha refuses to take his calls, and Meghan is in hospital preparing for the birth of her baby , unaware that she might be in danger. Adaptation of Michael Robotham's novel, starring Laura Carmichael and Jessica De Gouw.(n)

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: An actor who was initially involved in the play, but dropped out due to scheduling conflicts, comes back to haunt the production. Meanwhile, Georgia tells Anna that she is due to be a birthing partner for her friend, and Michael is blackmailed by one of his neighbours. Comedy, starring David Tennant and Michael Sheen.(n)

programme.title: Football Focus

Winning the Scare Games will make the difference between Mike staying at the campus or having to leave. Pixar's animated comedy prequel to Monsters Inc, with the voices of John Goodman, Billy Crystal, Steve Buscemi and Helen Mirren.(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. This time, all the contestants come from the world of journalism, as Owen Jones, Paris Lees, Kay Burley, Adam Boulton, Carole Malone, Quentin Letts, Amol Rajan and Victoria Derbyshire take part.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jade prepares to meet her birth mother for the first time. She's incredibly nervous, convinced she was given away because of her deafness, but Marty tries his best to reassure her. She faces her fears and heads to a cafe to meet her mum, finally uncovering the truth behind her past in the process. Meanwhile, Charlie throws himself back into work, but it's clear there is something else on his mind, and Faith attempts a conversation with Dylan but he pushes her away, claiming he needs some distance.(n)

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. This time, rapper Tinie Tempah and comedian Katherine Ryan pop by for a chat and a game of Musical VAR, Mo Farah takes on Jessica Ennis-Hill in The Sporting Fix, Peter takes Jamie Redknapp on an extreme retirement challenge and Ellie Goulding Saves Our Summer with an exclusive performance in The Garden Sessions.(n)

As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies.(n)

programme.title: The Secrets She Keeps
programme.desc: The Australian drama continues by delving into Agatha's childhood within the tight-knit, conservative Jehovah's Witness community, and a terrible tragedy that has left her emotionally scarred is revealed. Meanwhile, Hayden, who on special leave from the Navy, is becoming desperate when Agatha refuses to take his calls, and Meghan is in hospital preparing for the birth of her baby , unaware that she might be in danger. Adaptation of Michael Robotham's novel, starring Laura Carmichael and Jessica De Gouw.(n)

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: An actor who was initially involved in the play, but dropped out due to scheduling conflicts, comes back to haunt the production. Meanwhile, Georgia tells Anna that she is due to be a birthing partner for her friend, and Michael is blackmailed by one of his neighbours. Comedy, starring David Tennant and Michael Sheen.(n)

programme.title: Northern Ireland on The One Show
programme.desc: Jennie Browne and Joe Lindsay present reports from the magazine programme, including one man's ambitious plan to make the perfect brew with tea grown in Portaferry.(n)

programme.title: I May Destroy You
programme.desc: Arabella's obsession with fellow young author Della leads to unexpected progress on her book, while she continues staking out the scene of her attack. Kwame wades into uncharted waters with Tyrone and attempts to make amends with a disgruntled ex-flame, while Terry has some luck in both work and romance.(n)

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Residents of Hong Kong are living with a new reality - a draconian national security law made in China and imposed on the territory with no meaningful consultation. Pro-democracy activists call it the death of the one country, two systems principle established 23 years ago. HARDtalk's Stephen Sackur speaks to Ronnie Tong, once a pro-democracy politician, now a loyalist of the Beijing backed Hong Kong government. Has China just killed Hong Kong's special status?(n)

Musical remake, starring Quvenzhane Wallis, Jamie Foxx, Cameron Diaz and Rose Byrne.(n)

programme.title: The Perfect Man (2005)
programme.desc: Teenager Holly Hamilton grows tired of moving home every time her mother splits up with another boyfriend. When the family travels to New York, Holly is determined to stay in one place and vows to find her mum the ideal partner - even if she has to make him up. However, when the relationship starts to take off, Holly is faced with the impossible task of producing a perfect man to live up to her mother's inflated expectations. Romantic comedy, starring Hilary Duff, Heather Locklear and Chris Noth.(n)

programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: An unusual message appears in Chipping Cleghorn's local newspaper, revealing details of a forthcoming murder and, while at first glance it looks like a practical joke, the information proves to be deadly accurate. Miss Marple helps Inspector Craddock with his investigation and the pair soon uncover several likely suspects in the village. Feature-length drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Zoe Wanamaker, Elaine Paige, Virginia McKenna, Cherie Lunghi and Frances Barber.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: When Toyah calls round to offer support, Leanne turns on her, insisting she has no idea how she's feeling. Sally's appalled to learn from Cathy how Geoff locked Yasmeen in his magic box. And when Michael reveals that he's got a date with Grace at the bistro, Aggie warns him not to get involved.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick suggests to Leanne that they should go to a support group for parents of terminally ill children, Tim is shocked to find out that Sally has visited Yasmeen, and Roy apologises for upsetting Evelyn.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Yasmeen is horrified when she gets a phone call from Geoff, Leanne apologises to Toyah for the awful things she said, and Abi swerves a breakdown call from the twins' adoptive dad.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour DI Jack Frost suspects a man he arrested years before for a series of bizarre assaults on children could have returned to the area and recommenced his unsavoury activities. He tracks down the culprit, who swears he has turned over a new leaf, and decides not to charge him - a decision that shocks fellow sleuth DS Maud, whose misgivings are later justified in tragic fashion. Starring David Jason, Peter Gunn, Susannah Doyle, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Gwyneth Powell.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective is ordered to take a holiday for the benefit of his health, and with Hastings and Miss Lemon in tow, reluctantly heads for the tranquillity of Devon's coast, where his relaxation is soon ruined by the discovery of a socialite's murdered body on the beach. Several people had reason for wishing Arlena Stuart dead, but high on the list of suspects are her husband, teenage son and a troubled vicar. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser, Philip Jackson and Pauline Moran star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan as the genteel sleuth. A woman experiences an unsettling vision after moving into the home of her dreams - and it transpires she lived in the house as a child. Miss Marple investigates the history of the property and discovers it was connected with a baffling disappearance years before - which has never been solved. With Dawn French, Russ Abbot, Geraldine Chaplin, Phil Davis and Sarah Parish.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of Midsomer resident and biscuit factory boss Eric Calder leads Barnaby and Nelson to Copenhagen. The pair join forces with local female detectives Birgitte Poulsen and Anna Degn in the hunt for the killer, and during the course of their investigation they discover the businessman had numerous links to the capital. Anglo-Danish collaboration originally shown to mark Midsomer's 100th episode, guest starring Ann Eleonora Jorgensen (The Killing), Birgitte Hjort Sorensen (Borgen), Sanjeev Bhaskar and Joanna Scanlan, alongside Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The residents of Milton Cross, a feudal estate with a church, farms, houses and acres of land, depend on lord of the manor Edward Milton for their livelihoods. When local woman Sonia Woodley is stabbed to death in the churchyard two years after the death of her husband, bespoke tailor Gerald, Barnaby and Jones investigate, and the village begins to reveal its secrets. Detective drama, guest starring James Wilby, Gwyneth Strong and Karl Davies (formerly Emmerdale's Robert Sugden), with John Nettles and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A land dispute escalates during a cowboy fair run by the Wild West Society in the village of Ford Florey. Farmers Jack Fincher and Silas Burbage both claim to own a patch of wasteland known as the Swamp, but Dan Malko wants to buy it and create a travellers' site. Barnaby and Jones are called in when the feud leads to murder during the Wild West-style shoot-outs at the annual event. Guest starring Kenneth Cranham, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A woman's body is pulled from the river, reminding Frost that he investigated the death of her husband's first wife years ago. Unfortunately, it turns out that a key witness's statement went astray during the earlier case - which leads to Frost being suspended from duty. Discreetly continuing his enquiries, he is intrigued to learn the local priest recently heard a very disturbing confession. Starring David Jason, Eoin McCarthy, Anthony Calf, Kenneth Cope and Isla Blair.(n)

programme.title: Barging Round Britain with John Sergeant
programme.desc: The Regent's Canal runs through the heart of London linking the docks with the rest of Britain. John navigates the history-filled route, finding the East End's haunted Ragged School, and making and sampling a bottle or two of 'mother's ruin' along the way. The veteran broadcaster even finds time to stop off to feed the giraffes at London Zoo.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.desc: Robert teaches Angie to drive but it proves disastrous.(n)
