programme.desc: Mel calls on Joe for a favour when she pretends to her date, Travis, that she is an excellent cook, and is asked to put her money where her mouth is. Meanwhile, Lennox is shocked when she finds out that one of her friends has a crush on Ryder. Family comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Ryder turns to Joe for advice about investing in stocks and shares - but then fails to take him up on a tip, which turns out to be a profitable one. Meanwhile, Mel tries to hide her delight when Lennox and Haskell break up. Family comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Sam wants to give her relationship with Ben another chance. Bonnie is hired to act as Elle's new estate agent. Danny and Tucker volunteer for a charity ice-skating show that features Emma's favourite princess. Riley does her best to practise being a parent.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben takes Riley away for a hike in the woods to give her a break from an overprotective Danny, while Bonnie and Tucker search for evidence that may get Brad released from jail.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Amy try hanging out as friends when they spend Thanksgiving together at the aquarium. Howard reluctantly agrees to volunteer at a soup kitchen with Bernadette, Raj and Emily. Meanwhile, Penny discovers that Leonard knows more about her than she thought.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is driven to distraction when a tune becomes stuck in his head, while Amy and Dave arrange another date and Howard and Raj discover their band has a fan, but tracking him down turns out to be a mistake. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons, Mayim Bialik, Simon Helberg and Kunal Nayyar.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly decides their street needs another stop sign and pleads her case to the city comptroller. Barry panics when he realises he's neglected his college applications, while Erica struggles with her career choice.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam and his friends are recruited to the William Penn Academy baseball team. Barry remains in denial about his pitching skills, while Geoff's father interferes in his relationship with Erica.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Boyle enlists Peralta's help in tracking down his son's favourite toy for Christmas, but they find themselves accidentally facing off against a Latvian crime ring as a result. Back at the precinct, the team members get ready for the annual Christmas carol competition against their rivals at the Metropolitan Transit Authority. Mary Lynn Rajskub guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Part one of two. A prison van crash sees a mass escape of convicts take place and the entire squad has to track the villains down. Peralta and Santiago make a bet on which of them can bring in the most escaped prisoners, with the loser forced to move into the winner's apartment when they start living together. Former American football star Marshawn Lynch guests.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon falls ill, and his friends decide to go to a Planet of the Apes movie marathon to keep their distance, knowing he will be in a terrible mood. However, an unsuspecting Penny offers to nurse him. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Simon Helberg and Kaley Cuoco.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon feels threatened by a 15-year-old physics prodigy who is even cleverer than he is, and asks Leonard to help him discredit the precocious teenager's research. Johnny Galecki and Jim Parsons star.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Zander and Ryan begin working on a graphic novel, but Lennox takes all the fun out of the project when she assumes some editorial responsibilities. Meanwhile, Mel attempts to persuade Joe to consider waiting before embarking on a physical relationship with his new partner. Family comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel gets back in touch with a childhood friend, and as things progress romantically, Joe fears they could actually be relatives. Meanwhile, Lennox faces a moral dilemma when she discovers that her blog's new sponsor participates in unethical labour practices. Guest starring Trevor Donovan.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam unintentionally lets it slip that Barry never completed his community service requirement. Erica makes plans for her and Geoff to follow The Grateful Dead all summer, while Barry and his friends graduate from high school.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: The Goldbergs head on one final family vacation to Disneyland. Meanwhile, Erica is worried that Geoff is going to break up with her.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta and Diaz try to earn the respect of their idol, Lt Melanie Hawkins after accidentally disrupting one of her undercover cases. Boyle, Jeffords and Gina attempt to resolve a petty conflict between Scully and Hitchcock, while Holt loses Santiago's favourite pen. Guest starring Gina Gershon.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta and Diaz learn there's more to Lt Melanie Hawkins' team than meets the eye as they continue their search for the gang of bank robbers. Jeffords, Boyle and Santiago begin to suspect that Gina is harbouring a secret. Ryan Phillippe, Gina Gershon and Jason Mantzoukas guest star.(n)

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: Pilot episode of the comedy prequel to The Big Bang Theory about nine-year-old Sheldon Cooper. He possesses a once-in-a-generation mind capable of advanced mathematics and science, but finds that gift isn't always helpful growing up in East Texas, a land where church and football are king. Iain Armitage stars.(n)

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: To appease his mother, who worries that he might be lonely, Sheldon employs the techniques described in a self-help book from the library in an attempt to make a friend.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Elated at being named one of People magazine's '30 to Watch Under 30', Raj makes a pass at Penny and becomes very arrogant, making it hard for Leonard to maintain a positive perspective on his friend's new-found confidence. Guest starring Charlie Sheen.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is forced to confront his fear of driving when both Penny and Howard refuse to give him a lift to work. Desperate to be free of the obligation, Leonard and the gang intervene to discover why their friend never learnt to drive. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons and Johnny Galecki, with Octavia Spencer.(n)

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester.(n)

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The second commitment ceremony sees the couples return to the couch to explain all to the experts and decide whether to stay or leave the experiment.(n)

programme.title: Mike and Dave Need Wedding Dates (2016)
programme.desc: Two hard-partying brothers place an online advertisement to find the perfect dates for their sister's Hawaiian wedding. Hoping for a wild getaway, the boys instead find themselves out-hustled by an uncontrollable duo and calamity ensues. Comedy, starring Zac Efron, Anna Kendrick and Adam Devine.(n)

programme.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer
programme.desc: Sunnydale High is rocked by a series of strange supernatural events caused by the tortured ghosts of two lovers, who possess living people and force them to re-enact their final moments - with fatal results. As Willow plans an exorcism, Buffy is troubled by the similarities between the ghosts and her own tragic relationship with Angel, while Giles becomes convinced Jenny Calendar is trying to contact him from beyond the grave.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty wants to know why jelly won't set if chopped papaya is added to it, so he visits South Africa and discovers that the exotic fruit contains enzymes that can also help tenderise meat. In Hawaii, Kate Quilton visits America's only tuna auction to find out why some fish have dangerous levels of mercury, while others don't. She also visits a groundbreaking tech start-up in San Francisco that hopes to feed the world by growing fish flesh in a dish. Matt Tebbutt finds out why homemade ice cubes are cloudy, when the ones bought in shops are clear.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman heads to northern Tenerife to help Edinburgh couple John Clark and David Cameron find a dwelling that will eventually become their retirement home. With their wedding just weeks away, the pair have £100,000 to find somewhere that will accommodate a future mother-in-law as well as five visiting children.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps Bedfordshire couple Liz and Grahame Pink, who have saved hard for their dream holiday home in Lanzarote and have a budget of £63,000 to spend on a property.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Emma Greenfield and Daniel Halley search for a home on the Canary Island of Fuerteventura with a budget of £73,000. Presenter Jasmine Harman is on hand to show them a selection of properties on the market.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps Jay and Charlene, parents to one-year-old Kai, who bought and refurbished their two bed home in Romford, Essex but now want to upsize to Chelmsford. Meanwhile, Phil Spencer assists Jemma and Graham, who returned from Los Angeles three years ago and want to settle in Southend-on-Sea.(n)

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Liam Charles and Tom Allen welcome back the three best teams from both sets of heats, who join forces to face new rivals and their toughest challenges so far. For the first round, the teams are asked to create 24 perfectly identical classic kouign-amann and soaked babas in just four hours. In the second challenge, they must make a sugar showpiece inspired by insects incorporating 30 lollipops and 30 jelly sweets. Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden have to choose five of the teams to go through to the quarter finals.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Five people from Swindon take turns trying to throw the perfect dinner party. International DJ Mark Eteson hosts the first night and hopes his Ibiza-style menu featuring Bora Bora Beach brownies, paella and fishcakes will be a hit, before challenging his guests to a rave-off which he will judge himself.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Amateur dramatics enthusiast Michaela Kearns hosts the second party in Swindon, but admits she is not much of a cook. She hopes to dazzle her guests with a glamour-filled evening featuring melted chocolate with dipping treats, but anticipates she might not be able to persuade her fellow diners to take part in her planned show tune performance - complete with a full band.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Financial advisor Scott Hillier hosts Swindon's third party, hoping to impress his guests with a Wild West-themed evening. Tensions run high as Gill Bright awkwardly apologises to last night's host Michaela Kearns and Scott Hillier hopes to smooth things over by performing a rap.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Direct sales manager Sally Rachael hosts the fourth dinner party in Swindon, featuring a 'girls' night in' theme with mysteriously titled dishes and a quirky dress code that leaves her guests clueless and unhappy.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Alternative therapist Gill Bright hosts the final dinner party in Swindon and has plans for an 'upstairs downstairs' Edwardian-themed evening with sophisticated food followed by some circle dancing to Bob Marley.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Vale of Berkeley Guest House in Gloucestershire, where owners Harri and Claire Chiba hope to prove that their establishment offers the best value for money. However, two guests from rival B&Bs have an issue with the no-frills toilet, and another places a tricky request for 'anaemic toast' at the breakfast table.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to Artist Residence in Penzance, Cornwall, where proprietors Justin and Charlie hope to impress their rivals with a pottery class. However, one pair decide their room is too modern for their tastes, a duo are shocked to find hair in a shower filter and one guest seems underwhelmed by breakfast.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Third to host are Alec and Craig Coleman, who open the doors to the Edenfield Guest House in Blackpool and invite their fellow B&B owners to rate their establishment. However, on arrival one of the guests and his wife discover stains on the bedspread, while others feel the 'award-winning' food is not up to scratch.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the New Inn, Portland, Dorset, for the fourth day of the competition, where hosts Stacey and Spencer Cullum introduce their guests to a typical island activity - sandcastle building. Meanwhile, Claire Chiba quizzes Stacey about her breakfast hamper and says she is alienating customers, before Harri Chiba tells Justin that his B&B is too 'manufactured'.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The four sets of owners meet one last time to discover how much their rivals thought they should receive and to settle some scores. Two contestants attempt to explain away their negative feedback, and one contender finds herself unable to hold back her frustration, before the winner of the competition is revealed.(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Lost Family
programme.desc: The inside story of the royal family over three turbulent decades from the 1920s to the end of the Second World War. It begins just after the First World War, as monarchies throughout Europe have toppled, and most working men and many women now have the vote.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow patients at south west London's St George's hospital on royal wedding day. As Harry and Meghan tie the knot, patients and their relatives reflect on the meaning of lifelong love, devotion and commitment. Among those treated are David, 74, who is flown to St George's by air ambulance after falling from a ladder. Medics suspect internal bleeding and he is rushed to CT to assess the damage. His wife Lynda makes her way to the hospital as doctors try to prevent catastrophic blood loss.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow Aydigul, 38, who has suspected sepsis following major abdominal surgery. Her sister is at her bedside throughout, and discusses their childhood in Tajikistan and her extraordinary strength in the face of adversity. Plus, emergency consultant Ahmed rushes to save the life of a woman who has an abdominal aortic aneurysm.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Dougal takes over as Craggy Island's new milkman, but his predecessor, local womaniser Pat Mustard, returns to exact an explosive revenge on Ted for getting him sacked. Starring Ardal O'Hanlon and Dermot Morgan.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Dougal and Ted visit the mainland, where they bump into actor Richard Wilson - and discover the hard way that he does not take kindly to being confused with his comic alter ego Victor Meldrew. Dermot Morgan and Ardal O'Hanlon star.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the famous quiz, with team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson joined by guests Rhod Gilbert and Sara Pascoe. Countdown regular Susie Dent is assisted in Dictionary Corner by Johnny Vegas, while Rachel Riley provides the letters and numbers with a little help from Joey Essex.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow patients at south west London's St George's hospital on royal wedding day. As Harry and Meghan tie the knot, patients and their relatives reflect on the meaning of lifelong love, devotion and commitment. Among those treated are David, 74, who is flown to St George's by air ambulance after falling from a ladder. Medics suspect internal bleeding and he is rushed to CT to assess the damage. His wife Lynda makes her way to the hospital as doctors try to prevent catastrophic blood loss.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow Aydigul, 38, who has suspected sepsis following major abdominal surgery. Her sister is at her bedside throughout, and discusses their childhood in Tajikistan and her extraordinary strength in the face of adversity. Plus, emergency consultant Ahmed rushes to save the life of a woman who has an abdominal aortic aneurysm.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Dougal takes over as Craggy Island's new milkman, but his predecessor, local womaniser Pat Mustard, returns to exact an explosive revenge on Ted for getting him sacked. Starring Ardal O'Hanlon and Dermot Morgan.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw travel to Kent to pick up a very tired 1991 VW T4 Campervan. The vehicle's owner has recently had cancer of the brain and his wife is hoping the boys can help bring a ray of light to his life.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend head to Derbyshire to restore a 1971 Lamborghini Espada, whose owner hoped to return it to its former glory before ill health scuppered the project.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw restore a 1972 Ford Escort Mexico belonging to a Jersey resident with blood cancer and tuberculosis, but find some parts of the car are not salvageable.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Personal trainer Eve Booth hosts the first dinner party in Co Durham and Wearside, expressing concern that her starter of haggis and black pudding may not be to everyone's taste. Although her main course gets a lukewarm reception, the biggest threat to the evening seems to be fellow contestant Ken Brown's taste in jokes.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Interior designer and gypsy wedding supplier Paula Thompson hosts the second party in Co Durham and Wearside, and hopes to bring a touch of class to the proceedings with her 'black and bling' themed event. The evening's menu includes fresh lobster and hairy prawns, accompanied by a spot of belly dancing.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Nineteen-year-old languages student Donal Anand-Shaw hosts the third dinner party in Co Durham and Wearside, and decides to make the event black tie. He also arranges for a string quartet to play, and serves up a locally-sourced scallop starter.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Debt collector Eleri Watson hosts the fourth dinner party in Co Durham and Wearside. However, her efforts to organise an all-Welsh evening do not go according to plan when soil is found in the leek soup and lamb is served burned, dampening the guests' enthusiasm.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Businessman Ken Brown hosts the final dinner party in Co Durham and Wearside, and hopes to entertain his guests with a 'wear a wig' themed evening. After annoying his fellow contestants over the past week, he hopes to make amends with his unique entertainment, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Old Stables, Somerset, where Finnish-born Nina Dodd and her husband Andy welcome guests to their Scandinavian-style accommodation. While some are impressed by the immaculate interior, David and Erica Hensley are not pleased about the no-shoes policy.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to Rigsby's Guest House in Hertfordshire, where owner Morris Cockman entertains his rivals with a visit to a local zoo. Not all of his guests are impressed, and a slow breakfast service does not improve his chances of success.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: David and Erica Hensley host at the Fairway in Blackpool, where they hope to win over their guests with a spot of classic seaside entertainment. After taking their fellow contestants on a horror walk, the couple will be wary of any unexpected surprises when it comes to their scores.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Robin Hughes is the final host of the week in the converted Dent Station in the Yorkshire Dales. He organises a butchery competition and high-end food, before some home truths about the group dynamic are revealed.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The four sets of owners meet one last time to discover how much their rivals thought they should receive and to settle some scores. Robin Hughes' remarks from across the week are discussed, before the winner of the competition is revealed.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Charity manager Andrew Batsford hosts the first of a week of parties from Hastings, aiming to inspire his fellow contestants with a menu of dishes prepared using foraged ingredients, including sea broccoli and stinging nettles. However, if the food fails to win them over, he hopes a dynamic Wham!-themed evening of festivities will do the trick.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the second day of the competition in Hastings, East Sussex, and retired solicitor Adrian Carter is hoping to impress his guests with a Moroccan-themed evening. However, disaster strikes when he neglects his tagine, and his rendition of a Rod Stewart classic does not go down well either.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sally-Anne Keller hosts a German-themed dinner party on the third night in Hastings, East Sussex, and requires her guests to dress, sing and dance accordingly. Charity manager Andrew Batsford explains how the principles of anarchism and punk can be applied to football, while retired solicitor Adrian Carter dresses as the 19th-century statesman Otto von Bismarck and raises eyebrows with his jokes.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hairdresser Kate Eadon hosts the fourth dinner party in Hastings, East Sussex, with a menu styled on a gourmet pub meal. Adrian Carter gets himself in hot water, Sally-Anne Keller reveals a surprise celebrity crush, and the host recounts her own encounter with a famous funnyman.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The confident Stelios Michael hosts the final night in Hastings, East Sussex, hoping to impress his guests with a Greek-inspired evening. The diners get involved when they receive a masterclass in the country's traditional dance, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Architect Martin Pease plans an unusual home for his family where everything is white and open plan. He calls upon his experience of designing commercial buildings to build the house and uses materials and systems more commonly used in office projects. The biggest risk is that his house may look more like a car showroom than a comfortable retreat.(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A selection of stories examining the effects of substance abuse on the NHS, including Coventry paramedics treating a suspected drink-driver who has crashed his car.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Forty-year-old Tania is rushed to St George's with life-threatening injuries, having been knocked over while crossing a road, while semi-professional footballer Lilli arrives in A&E after being elbowed in the face during a match. Seventy-seven-year-old Edwin is rushed to resus bleeding from a tumour on his neck, and as the medical team examines the wound, his partner Jeanette explains how their romance blossomed in later life. When a maxillofacial specialist arrives to plan the next stage of Edwin's treatment, it forces his family to confront the very painful truth.(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: This edition focuses on the growing lack of respect officers face on the street, with a mass confrontation between teenagers in Northampton having worrying implications for PC Allan Whenmouth. A 15-year-old is arrested for assaulting a police officer in Corby town centre, while rookie PC Wioletta Britten reveals she experienced her first assault after working on response for only a few months.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Bridgeport, Connecticut, to visit Cafe Tavolini, which is being neglected by husband and wife managers teetering on the edge of divorce. The estranged couple's two children are struggling to save the business from their parents' disputes, and the visiting chef tries to shock the pair into stopping the restaurant from becoming a casualty of their quarrel.(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A selection of stories examining the effects of substance abuse on the NHS, including Coventry paramedics treating a suspected drink-driver who has crashed his car.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Forty-year-old Tania is rushed to St George's with life-threatening injuries, having been knocked over while crossing a road, while semi-professional footballer Lilli arrives in A&E after being elbowed in the face during a match. Seventy-seven-year-old Edwin is rushed to resus bleeding from a tumour on his neck, and as the medical team examines the wound, his partner Jeanette explains how their romance blossomed in later life. When a maxillofacial specialist arrives to plan the next stage of Edwin's treatment, it forces his family to confront the very painful truth.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Dougal and Ted visit the mainland, where they bump into actor Richard Wilson - and discover the hard way that he does not take kindly to being confused with his comic alter ego Victor Meldrew. Dermot Morgan and Ardal O'Hanlon star.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps man to create a fun bedroom for his two daughters. She creates a pinboard using a bold vintage fabric, and a garland from the felt offcuts. Plus, the girls help to personalise some jewellery boxes.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Mother and daughter Sue and Stacey Jones, who have a budget of £72,000, search for a holiday home that will also generate a rental income on Spain's Costa del Sol. Laura Hamilton shows them a selection of properties available for purchase.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien restore eight items, including a pine blanket chest, a pair of battlefield telephones and some gothic church windows.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are in Wales, where they pick up and restore a dentist's table and a barber's chair, and make a coffee table out of parquet flooring.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Angel Adoree advises Jonathan and Michael on how to make their chateau wedding business work. Then it's crunch time for the pair as they try to persuade an American couple to book a big-money wedding with them. Meanwhile, Phil and Angelina clear their garden for a wedding anniversary party, but struggle to dispose of the debris. Elsewhere, Johnny and Ashley view two properties as they search for a dream chateau.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets engaged couple Jamie Ashton and Joanne Scurr who want to build a family home in Hull without compromising on design, but who are struggling to fund both a new build and their approaching wedding, despite the groom-to-be gaining some great deals through his connections as an architect.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at No 33 in Hunstanton, Norfolk, where owner Janie Thompson and housekeeper Gemma Layfield hope their quirky design and brand of luxury will win the day.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to La Tavola Calda in Nuneaton, Warwickshire, where father and son team Mick and Chris Emanuele run a basic B&B above their Italian restaurant.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the Lansdowne in the Cotswolds for the third day of the contest, where Janie and Gemma like the rooms, but Mick and Chris are determined to find fault.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The last visit is to the Ivybridge Hotel and Spa in Fishguard Bay, Pembrokeshire, where Cheryle and Anthony are unimpressed to be housed in an annexe.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to thrash out their issues. No.33 Hunstanton is up first, as hosts Janie and Gemma quiz Mark and Jan over their privacy problems. Next, father and son duo Mick and Chris Emanuele from La Tavola Calda want to put Cheryle and Anthony's sleep issues to bed. Eventually, matters are resolved and the winner is announced.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Drive It
programme.desc: After deciding to enter a ploughing competition, Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove fix up a vintage tractor and plough combination and then learn to to use it.(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A poodle called Diamond has been referred for a total hip replacement, and another, with the same owner, needs a new knee. Noel Fitzpatrick operates on both dogs on the same day, and doesn't finish until 3am. Hopity, a 12-week-old Balinese kitten, has a deformity on one of her back legs, which she could end up losing, while the operation itself is a huge risk. Hazell has brought in Dandi, a five month-old German shepherd who is lame in both front legs.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge visits Philip and Angelina and helps them build a bridge at their chateau - will Dick's design work? Jonathan and Michael test the services they provide at their chateau by inviting Michael's old college mates for the weekend, while Jonny and Ashley continue the search for their dream chateau.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Jo and Shaun Bennett, a couple with a love of gothic architecture and horror movies, plan to use their budget of £400,000 to build an Addams Family-style house, complete with a stained-glass window, a stone fireplace and an oak staircase featuring gargoyles. However, completing the five-bedroom home on limited funds proves difficult.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw take on a 1983 Citroen 2CV after its owner was unable to continue its restoration after being diagnosed with stage 4 throat cancer.(n)

programme.title: Sarah Millican: Control Enthusiast
programme.desc: A stand-up performance by the comedian, including topics such as road rage, IBS, her favourite word and how she lets her husband know 'tonight's the night'. There are also stories about 'Rescue Men', passing wind in hospital pants, what can happen at a bra fitting and how she feels about her belly.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy game show, with team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson joined by comedians Katherine Ryan and Rob Beckett. Comedian Greg Davies is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent, while Rachel Riley provides the letters and numbers.(n)

programme.title: Brojects

programme.title: Brojects
programme.desc: Kevin and Andrew Buckles decide to build a retreat from the retreat, in the form of an igloo on the lake with all of the amenities of a luxury hotel.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond debate which company has made the greatest number of exceptional vehicles, and James May finds a way of transporting caravans to campsites without inconveniencing other road users. The Top Gear track plays host to a replica of one of the world's most famous rally cars, and broadcaster Chris Evans - some five years before he was announced as the show's new presenter - is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein: From Venice to Istanbul
programme.desc: The chef's gastronomic road trip from Venice to Istanbul through the countries of the former Byzantine Empire is nearing its end as he leaves southern Greece for the shores of western Turkey. On the Cesme Peninsula Rick enjoys creamy Armola cheese matured in goat skins, Urla's famous red wine and the ultimate Turkish breakfast. He also cooks the country's iconic dish sultan's delight - a mouth-watering lamb stew on a bed of aubergine puree.(n)

programme.title: Border Interceptors

programme.title: Border Interceptors
programme.desc: Officer James O'Hearne has to deal with a passenger who has no passport and no form of identification, while a Chinese lady who has just arrived from Toronto faces being deported.(n)

programme.title: Cop Car Workshop
programme.desc: The work taking place in one of Britain's leading police car workshops, as bespoke vehicles are built and repairs made to ones that have been out in the field. In the first episode, John Heussi finds himself in need of a new riot van, and stress-testing the old one helps him design a new one.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The hosts bid to prove that cars can be art by filling a gallery in Middlesbrough with motoring-related works, including a few creations of their own. They also resort to unusual promotional techniques to attract more visitors to their exhibition. Back on the track, Jeremy Clarkson tests British supercar the Noble M600. Jenson Button drives the Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve and the team battle through the jungle into the heart of Indonesian Borneo. Here, a remote mountain range guards caves, holding secrets that could rewrite the history of man.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef embarks on another round of extended culinary breaks, visiting markets, restaurants, wineries, cafes and bars, beginning with Portuguese capital Lisbon. The culinary effects of Portugal's historic explorations are still seen in its dishes with hints of cinnamon and freshly chopped coriander from the east and tomatoes and chillies from the Americas. Rick is inspired to cook salt cod fritters and an almond tart, before heading for the St Anthony's Day Parade and Sardine Festival.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson spares no expense when he tests the BMW X6. James May joins Margaret Calvert, one of Britain's road-sign designers, to test-drive the Vauxhall Insignia VXR, and Richard Hammond tries out a £340,000 Lexus supercar. Plus, musician Seasick Steve is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond take on a challenge to find an alternative to the expensive diesel-fuelled hatchbacks driven by British police officers. Alan Carr and Justin Lee Collins take up the gauntlet as the Stars in a Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: It's time for the grand final, which features peas, teas and absent keys, as Ed Gamble, Jo Brand, Katy Wix and Rose Matafeo vie to go home with Greg Davies's massive golden face.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Four famous faces go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games across the week, with comedians Nish Kumar and Al Murray, Radio 1 DJ Clara Amfo and broadcaster Anneka Rice the first contestants. A daily winner is declared following a quick-fire round at the end of each show, and the weekly scores are tallied resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday, when the victor chooses between a House of Games dartboard and a branded dressing gown.(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Doon Mackichan, Jimmy Carr, Dara O Briain and Alan Davies wax lyrical on subjects including endings. Comedy panel game, with Stephen Fry awarding points for the answers he finds most interesting.(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sean Lock, Bill Bailey and Jo Brand join Alan Davies as host Stephen Fry poses more mind-boggling questions on subjects beginning with the letter E, including empire.(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Sara Pascoe introduces a special festive edition of the comedy show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, warming up the audience and then introducing routines by Chris McCausland and musical comedy double act Flo & Joan.(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry hosts another round of the peculiar panel quiz and finds out how much Sandi Toksvig, Bill Bailey, Susan Calman and regular panellist Alan Davies know about places that start with J, awarding points for the most interesting answers.(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Fresh Meat and Bad Education star Jack Whitehall makes his debut on the obscure general knowledge quiz, joining regular panellist Alan Davies and comedians Jimmy Carr and Cal Wilson to answer Stephen Fry's fiendish questions about joints.(n)

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies, aka The Taskmaster, sets challenges including trying to paint the best picture of a horse while riding a horse, melon-eating and bath-emptying to Roisin Conaty, Tim Key, Romesh Ranganathan, Frank Skinner and Josh Widdicombe, ultimately deciding who wins. Alex Horne acts as Davies' right-hand-man, coaxing the contestants to reveal how comic, creative and seriously competitive they are.(n)

programme.title: The Churchills
programme.desc: Historian David Starkey describes how Winston Churchill drew on the lessons of his ancestor John to lead the UK through the Second World War. At two key moments, the prime minister's strategic sense helped avert disaster, and in both decisions, the influence of the Duke of Marlborough is apparent. Starkey argues that it was Winston's ability to control events by giving them a narrative meaning that enabled him to shape world history long after his fall from power.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson embarks on a 45-mile walk across Wiltshire, from Avebury to Stonehenge, telling the story of the remarkable development that occurred in the latter days of the Neolithic era. His route over chalk downlands and Salisbury Plain takes him through one of the greatest concentrations of prehistoric sites in Europe.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: A selection of archive footage from the arrival of the so-called beat boom in 1964, which catered to the art-school craze for rhythm and blues. Featuring performances by Tom Jones, the Kinks, Manfred Mann, the Rolling Stones, John Lee Hooker and the Pretty Things.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Folk performances by artists including Pete Seeger, Tim Buckley, Joan Baez, Bob Dylan and Leonard Cohen.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Music from the Top of the Pops archives, featuring performances by acts including the Foundations, Procol Harum, the Bee Gees, the Rolling Stones, and Dave Dee, Dozy, Beaky, Mick and Tich.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Music from the flower power era, featuring bands including Herman's Hermits, the Crazy World of Arthur Brown, the Alan Price Set, Manfred Mann and the Rolling Stones.(n)

programme.title: Steam Train Britain
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of three preserved steam railway lines - the South Devon Railway heritage line, the Ffestiniog and Welsh Highlands Railway and the Great Central Railway. Sports scientist Megan Smith has a dream of tending the fire of a steam locomotive on the Ffestiniog & Welsh Highland Railway, and she stokes the bolier of NG143 and sets off along the 25 miles from Caernarfon to Porthmadog.(n)

programme.title: Steam Train Britain
programme.desc: At the Ffestiniog and Welsh Highlands Railway, manager Stephen Greig is trying to ensure that all goes to plan ahead of the opening of the new station at Caernarfon.(n)

programme.title: Steam Train Britain
programme.desc: At South Devon, engineer Ray Lee is working to bring a loco back in to service. There is also a visit to the SS Kingswear paddle steamer In Dartmouth, the oldest vessel of its kind.(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The surprising preventive and curative 'medicine' developed by certain species, including the hippopotamus, which secretes a red slime to act as a highly efficient sun screen.(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: Two families with remarkable powers of regenerating unusually large body parts easily - salamanders, which do so when a large chunk, such as the tail or a limb, has been bitten off by a predator, and male moose, which grow and shed antlers every year.(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of wild animals that have made a home in cities, including leopards on the streets of Mumbai, peregrine falcons hunting among New York's skyscrapers, hyenas that live peacefully alongside the people of Harar, macaques in Jaipur, and langurs in Jodhpur, which are regarded as having great religious significance by the citizens. The programme also examines how some animals are struggling to survive in urban environments, and questions how cities can be built to serve their needs as well as those of the human inhabitants.(n)

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: The country's diverse wildlife, beginning by exploring how rural Chinese peasants in the south have developed a relationship with the region's animal inhabitants - including how water buffalo are used in agriculture, and tamed cormorants help their owners to fish.(n)

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: The documentary series exploring China's wildlife continues with a journey to the Yunnan Province, where parallel mountain ranges channel mighty rivers from Tibet southward to the tropics, and the landscape provides a home to diverse species. While the mountain forests are home to red pandas, the valleys below contain jungles inhabited by gibbons, sunbirds and elephants.(n)

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: Wildlife living on the Tibetan plateau - an inhospitable area the size of western Europe - including brown bears, yaks, giant sheep, snow leopards and the chiru or Tibetan antelope.(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Footage of animals that live on the coast, including sea lions working in packs to hunt tuna in the shallows and moray eels that venture out of the water to seek out food on dry rocks. Puffins must brave predators to bring a mouthful of food back to their young and king penguins go without food for a month while they moult. Plus, footage of the bizarre Leaping Blenny, a fish that hates the water.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: A selection of archive footage from the arrival of the so-called beat boom in 1964, which catered to the art-school craze for rhythm and blues. Featuring performances by Tom Jones, the Kinks, Manfred Mann, the Rolling Stones, John Lee Hooker and the Pretty Things.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Folk performances by artists including Pete Seeger, Tim Buckley, Joan Baez, Bob Dylan and Leonard Cohen.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Music from the Top of the Pops archives, featuring performances by acts including the Foundations, Procol Harum, the Bee Gees, the Rolling Stones, and Dave Dee, Dozy, Beaky, Mick and Tich.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Music from the flower power era, featuring bands including Herman's Hermits, the Crazy World of Arthur Brown, the Alan Price Set, Manfred Mann and the Rolling Stones.(n)

programme.title: Royal Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: The mysterious death of the Duke of Kent in a 1942 plane crash, which has led to accusations of the Royal family erasing him from history.(n)

programme.title: Royals on the Frontline
programme.desc: An unprecedented insight into the Royals and the armed services that fight in their name, from the early 20th century to modern-day conflicts such as Afghanistan. The first episode focuses on Prince Philip and Queen Elizabeth.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Investigating what happened to a pirate city in Jamaica, a grim gulag in Croatia and a Louisiana plantation caught up in the aftermath of the American Civil War.(n)

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The untold secret story of war production that shaped the Second World War, beginning with how the German aviation industry came to dominate the skies of Europe.(n)

programme.title: The Churchills
programme.desc: Historian David Starkey describes how Winston Churchill drew on the lessons of his ancestor John to lead the UK through the Second World War. At two key moments, the prime minister's strategic sense helped avert disaster, and in both decisions, the influence of the Duke of Marlborough is apparent. Starkey argues that it was Winston's ability to control events by giving them a narrative meaning that enabled him to shape world history long after his fall from power.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson embarks on a 45-mile walk across Wiltshire, from Avebury to Stonehenge, telling the story of the remarkable development that occurred in the latter days of the Neolithic era. His route over chalk downlands and Salisbury Plain takes him through one of the greatest concentrations of prehistoric sites in Europe.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: It was 30 years after the Romans invaded Britain that they were ready to take on the challenge of conquering the Lake District. With the toughest landscape they had encountered in the nation, peopled by a rebellious tribe, it was no small task, but within a few years, the whole of Lakeland was under their control. Tony Robinson tackles the journey by himself, walking 50 miles from Penrith to Ambleside and on to the Irish Sea at Ravenglass, discovering that the Romans encountered beauty and danger in equal measure.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson takes a four-day trek along the coastline between Plymouth in Devon and Falmouth, learning about the thriving smuggling trade in Cornwall during the late 18th century. The area's tiny secretive harbours, beaches and secluded coves were ideal for illicit imports such as tobacco and brandy, and the business was so huge it threatened the national economy. Along his route, visiting such beauty spots as Lantic Bay and Polperro, he encounters all sorts of reminders of the trade and meets descendants of those involved.(n)

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: The true story of Franz Stigler, a German Luftwaffe pilot in the Second World War who risked his own life to save the crew of an American B-17 bomber.(n)

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: While Nazi scientists were conducting brutal experiments in an attempt to create an Aryan master race, a team of zoologists were overseeing their own bizarre science trials.(n)

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: A program set up by the Special Operations Executive, the forerunner to MI6, to undermine German and Japanese officers by spraying them with a foul-smelling substance.(n)

programme.title: WW2 - Battles for Europe
programme.desc: Eye-witness veteran testimony from men who served under fire on the frontline, taking viewers to the heart of iconic moments in the history of warfare. The first episode looks at the Allied invasion of Europe, which began on June 6 1944 - D-Day. British, American and Canadian troops stormed the beaches of Normandy in what would become known as The Longest Day.(n)

programme.title: WW2 - Battles for Europe
programme.desc: This edition examines the efforts of the British and Canadians who fought elite German divisions to capture the city of Caen, the key to breaking out of Normandy.(n)

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn explores St Pancras station in London and its sister hotel - built to show off the might of the Midlands, and learns about Castle Howard station in North Yorkshire.(n)

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn rides up Mount Snowdon's railway to visit the summit station, and discovers how a glass structure envelopes Strasbourg's 19th-century station.(n)

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn visits the former HQ of London Underground, 55 Broadway, which was the capital's first skyscraper, and sees the timber-framed Barmouth Bridge in north Wales.(n)

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn visits the architecture of the Stockton & Darlington railway, the first passenger steam line in the world and learns how Essex's enormous Chappel Viaduct was built.(n)

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn visits Swindon's railway village, which became home to Great Western's enormous works and he also explores the dark and secret past of Milan's station.(n)

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Documentary following the exploits of a family-run crew of heavy-haulage specialists as they transport the heaviest, longest and most precious locomotives around the world. Terry and Jason have been called in to move legendary steam locomotive Britannia. The 68-year-old vehicle has undergone a major restoration in Crewe and is scheduled to start running at the Severn Valley Railway.(n)

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Rene, Hans and the Colonel are taken to the chateau's dungeons for interrogation at the hands of Herr Flick and his fiendish organ music. Meanwhile, the British airmen prepare to make their escape by balloon. Wartime comedy, starring Gorden Kaye, Richard Gibson and Richard Marner.(n)

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: First episode of the Second World War comedy set in German-occupied France about the misadventures of womanising cafe owner Rene Artois, whose cafe is commandeered by the Resistance to shelter two airmen. Starring Gorden Kaye, Carmen Silvera and Vicki Michelle.(n)

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Herr Flick is in the village trying to trace a valuable painting, so Rene and the Colonel decide to smuggle it out with the two British airmen who are disguised unconvincingly as Germans.(n)

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris Tarrant returns for more train journeys through some of the world's most challenging terrain. He heads to the Arctic for a trip along the Alaska Railroad, but as he arrives in the port of Seward, it's not a great start as there are no trains running, so he ends up hitching a ride with a track inspector's car. He continues his journey on a freight train heading for Anchorage, Alaska's biggest city, and finally heads north on the Aurora passenger train to the town of Healy. Along the way he checks out the tiny village of Eklutna, which has great importance for the indigenous inhabitants, and goes for an intrepid fishing trip on a thawing frozen lake.(n)

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The story of Hitler's personal secretary Martin Bormann and the efforts to track him down. With insufficient evidence to confirm the Nazi official's death, the International Military Tribunal at Nuremberg tried him in absentia during 1946 and eventually sentenced him to death. Despite extensive efforts, he was never found and was eventually presumed to have died in May 1945.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Burton Constable Hall near Hull, which is home to a collection of treasures. Objects brought in to be valued include a ship's anchor that was found in a garden pond, a medal awarded to sailor for helping to recover Captain Scott's ship from the Antarctic in 1901, some 17th-century flat pack furniture, and a flimsy booklet that proved to be a profitable investment.(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The veteran naturalist reflects on the remarkable abilities that some plants and animals possess, such as the electric eel that helped inspire the battery and flowers that 'talk' to bees using electrical signals.(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the emperor penguin and the wood frog, both of which survive in conditions so cold they seem to defy the laws of nature. The emperor is the only penguin able to breed on Antarctic sea ice during winter, while the wood frog can survive up to 65 per cent of its body mass freezing during its hibernation.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the first leg of his second journey, Michael Portillo heads to Kirkcaldy in Fife, finding out along the way how the mill town of Preston in Lancashire gave birth to the Temperance Movement. In Blackpool, he enjoys a performance in the Tower Ballroom and discovers the railways had a direct impact on the town's birth as a resort. After a stroll along the pier with local comedian Joey Blower, Michael visits Morecambe Bay.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the second leg of his journey from Preston to Kirkcaldy, returning to the historic Settle-Carlisle line he helped save from dereliction when he was Minister of State for Transport in the 1980s. Here, engineer Tony Feshini describes what life was like for the people who originally built Ribblehead Viaduct, before Michael visits Dent, Cumbria, home to the highest railway station in England.(n)

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris travels through Argentina, starting his journey at British-built railway station in Buenos Aires and taking an overnight train to the pampas lowlands. In the major port of Bahia Blanca, Chris meets four veterans of the Falklands War and finds out what the locals now think about the British, before ending his journey by riding La Trochita, known in English as the Old Patagonian Express.(n)

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of a North Yorkshire-based, family-run historic car auction business, which uncovers some remarkably rare vehicles in the picturesque village of Thornton-le-Dale.(n)

programme.title: Open All Hours

programme.title: Jamie's Quick & Easy Food

Next up, the enterprising chef rustles up a sausage and apple bake - a family favourite in the making - and to finish off, prepares cherry chard rice - so quick and pretty, he regards it as a 'total joy' of a dish.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Manuel and Charlotte looks for a contemporary home for a construction worker and his partner, while Shaun helps a couple find a home that they can also rent out.(n)

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Mother-and-daughter duo Linda and Charlotte Rodriguez help sewing enthusiast Marilyn and her husband Gary Davey Herbert find a holiday home in Alicante on their 230,000-euro budget.(n)

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almeria, Sharon and Steve attempt to find a property for music lovers Bill and Christine from Hampshire, who enjoy writing songs together and singing them in bars.(n)

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almeria, Sharon Garner and son Shaun's next customers are couple Geoff and Anita, who are hoping to realise Geoff's lifelong ambition of retiring to Spain. After nearly 40 years of marriage, living by the coast in Southend-on-Sea, they're looking for a change of scenery, with a budget of 200,000 euros. In Alicante, husband and wife team Sophie and Damon have their work cut out finding an apartment for Yorkshire couple Graham and Lisa. programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

programme.title: The Man with the 10-Stone Testicles

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

During an afternoon playing crazy golf 'with a twist', simmering tension is revealed between Charlotte and Stephen, which erupts at dinner.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The last visit of the week is to the Pheasant hotel in Brill, Buckinghamshire, where professional chef Stephen Orr hopes his top-level place will win the day. On arrival, Adam and Susan are bowled over by the beautiful views and all the guests are surprised by the quality of the rooms. programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Law & Order

programme.title: Law & Order

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A controversial talk show host is shot dead in the street and suspicion initially falls on a woman who was suing him for sexual harassment. However, later evidence leads the team to the victim's wife, who stands to inherit $20million, and a stalker who claims to be having an affair with her. Crime drama, starring Sam Waterston.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Two room-mates are found bludgeoned to death in their flat, and the handyman is the prime suspect. As his options run out, he takes a hostage, forcing Southerlyn into a course of action that could jeopardise her career. Award-winning police drama, guest starring Gene Silvers, and Elisabeth Rohm.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate a state senator suspected of involvement in the disappearance of one of his aides. Their progress is hindered by a lack of evidence, but when her body is found in a marsh, the case turns into a murder inquiry. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs throws himself back into his work following his near-death experience, updating his appearance and tackling a new risky case alongside DEA agent Luis Mitchell, who wants help bringing down a drugs cartel. Concerned about his colleague's apparent enthusiasm to re-enter the field, DiNozzo discovers Mitchell is the son of a former NCIS agent who gave his life trying to save Gibbs' late wife and daughter, and this attachment is causing Gibbs to break his own rule against personal involvement in a case. However, a significant danger is posed by Benson Long, the cartel's head, and Mitchell's top target.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Marine is found murdered in Quantico, mere hours after calling Gibbs to discuss a possible case. In response, Bishop and McGee go undercover as a married couple to run surveillance on a lieutenant and his wife, who may be connected to the death. Starring Mark Harmon, Emily Wickersham and Michael Weatherly.(n)

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The notorious Tri-State Sniper appears to have claimed another victim when a petty officer is shot dead in a crowded park. However, when the team discovers the killer in question was already in custody on the day of the shooting, it becomes clear a copy-cat is on the loose. As their investigation progresses, the agents learn the victim was recently found guilty of placing a camera in the female changing rooms of the USS Camden, earning himself plenty of enemies - but did any of them hate him enough to kill?(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy Lieutenant JAG lawyer - who appeared to have a happy life - is found dead by her parents in an apparent suicide. Gibbs and the team interview family and friends from her past and present in a bid to unravel the facts.(n)

programme.title: Fred Dinenage: Murder Casebook
programme.desc: The presenter explores the case of John Straffen, who killed two young girls in the summer of 1951. He was found to be unfit to plead and committed to Broadmoor Hospital, but during a brief escape from the facility in 1952 he killed again. It was determined that the cause of his behaviour was severe brain damage, traced back to an attack of encephalitis as a child in India. When he died in 2007, he was the longest-serving prisoner in Britain.(n)

programme.title: A Handful of Dust (1989)
programme.desc: A couple's life is disrupted by a penniless woman who arrives for the weekend, setting in motion a disastrous sequence of events. Drama, starring Judi Dench, whose performance won her a Bafta award, and James Wilby.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Documentary exploring areas of the capital, beginning with Shooters Hill, Charlton, Greenwich and Blackheath.(n)

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White walks above and alongside two large bodies of water - Ladybower Reservoir and Windermere.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Amanda Lamb helps a couple search for a dream holiday home on the Greek islands of Paros and Naxos, including a dwelling on the beach, a chic urban home and a beautiful farmhouse with seven stunning sun-terraces.(n)

programme.title: The Bionic Vet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick prepares to amputate a corgi's cancerous paw and replace it with a prosthetic foot, a procedure that has only been carried out on two other dogs. He also catches up with a puppy that was suffering from a painful bone deformity, and treats a barn owl brought into the surgery with a broken wing.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring Canary Wharf and Southbank. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: The Second World War saw men from all classes unite to fight the Nazis, and this programme meets some of the last surviving pilots of the Battle of Britain. Women, who had been excluded from many jobs, worked in everything from anti-aircraft gun batteries to steel-making factories. The Blitz saw rescue workers risk all to save those trapped in bombed buildings. For many of the era's last survivors, the defiant spirit that brought the nation together remains one of their proudest moments, as this documentary reveals.(n)

programme.title: Oxford Street Revealed
programme.desc: The British Transport Police deal with an injured passenger and two disgruntled tourists. Meanwhile, a team of miners aims to prevent a billion-pound London Underground project from hitting the buffers, and a couple celebrate their first anniversary.(n)

programme.title: The Repo Man
programme.desc: In recession-hit Britain, many people have turned to finance companies to purchase items over a fixed period of time. This documentary follows the life and work of bailiff Sean James as he confronts debtors across the nation who fail to keep up their repayments.(n)

programme.title: Pirates of Somalia (2017)
programme.desc: In 2008, rookie journalist Jay Bahadur forms a plan to embed himself among the pirates of Somalia, and provides a rare profile of these men and the forces that drive them. Fact-based drama, starring Al Pacino and Evan Peters.(n)

programme.title: Felicity (1978)
programme.desc: A teenager in a remote Catholic girls' school seeks solace from her repressive life in reading erotic novels and having a secret love affair with one of her classmates. A trip to Hong Kong organised by her parents opens her eyes to more experiences - both good and bad. Australian drama, starring Glory Annen and Jody Hanson.(n)

programme.title: Common
programme.desc: One-off drama from Jimmy McGovern, exploring the possible consequences of the UK's Joint Enterprise law, by which more than one person can be charged with the same offence. Seventeen-year-old Johnjo gives his cousin Tony and some friends a lift to a pizza parlour for food, unaware their real intention is to 'sort out' a local loudmouth. Things soon get out of hand, however, and an innocent bystander is fatally stabbed - and despite being in the car all the time, Johnjo finds himself charged with murder along with everyone else. Nico Mirallegro heads a cast that also includes Susan Lynch, Daniel Mays, Jodhi May, Robert Pugh and Michelle Fairley.(n)

programme.title: Fox
programme.desc: Joey tries to settle the problem with Liz, and Kenny prepares for the most important fight of his career. Guest starring Patricia Quinn (The Rocky Horror Picture Show).(n)

programme.title: Cup Fever (1965)
programme.desc: A crooked councillor tries to make life difficult for a children's football team, so they will lose a match against his son's side. However, help is at hand from Manchester United manager Matt Busby. Children's drama, starring David Lodge and Busby as himself, with cameos from a host of football stars.(n)

programme.title: Eagle Rock (1964)
programme.desc: A boy attempts to climb a mountain on his own, but when he gets into serious trouble, he needs the help of his friends. Family drama, starring Pip Rolls and Tony Paul.(n)

programme.title: A Handful of Dust (1989)
programme.desc: A couple's life is disrupted by a penniless woman who arrives for the weekend, setting in motion a disastrous sequence of events. Drama, starring Judi Dench, whose performance won her a Bafta award, and James Wilby.(n)

programme.title: Blind Corner (1964)
programme.desc: A composer's wife learns her husband plans to cut her out of his will, so conspires with her lover to murder him first. Crime drama, starring William Sylvester and Barbara Shelley.(n)

programme.title: The Slipper and the Rose (1976)
programme.desc: A girl is reduced to being the downtrodden servant of her cruel stepmother after her father's death. With a little help from her fairy godmother, she wins the heart of a handsome prince, who is desperately trying to avoid being forced into a political marriage by his parents. Musical version of Cinderella, starring Gemma Craven and Richard Chamberlain.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: British house-hunters Ric and Nita Halstead, who are seeking a dream home in Miami, receive expert advice before buying. Amanda Lamb shows them round an art deco house, a Mediterranean-style villa and a Florida ranch.(n)

programme.title: Earth Cycle
programme.desc: Simon Parker charts the seasonal cycles of the natural world as he journeys through Scandinavia on a bicycle, wild camping, seeing animal migrations and sampling local food.(n)

programme.title: Capital Conversation
programme.desc: London-based current affair series, with Michael Hayman.(n)

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Helen seeks a boyfriend for her socially inept elder sister Laura, so invites nervous horticulturalist Mike to a party. The pair discover they share a dislike of being fixed up, so depart together to prevent Helen objecting to them leaving. Bob Larbey's comedy, starring Judi Dench and Michael Williams as middle-aged romantics.(n)

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Helen and Phil are keen to know how things went between unlikely lovebirds Laura and Mike, so both end up invited to lunch - where they relate conflicting stories. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Michael Williams.(n)

programme.title: Callan
programme.desc: Spy drama, starring Edward Woodward as an embittered secret agent.(n)

programme.title: Music Within (2007)
programme.desc: The true story of Richard Pimentel, a brilliant public speaker with a troubled past, who returns from Vietnam severely hearing-impaired and finds a new purpose in his landmark efforts on the behalf of Americans with disabilities. Biographical drama, starring Ron Livingston, Melissa George and Michael Sheen.(n)

programme.title: Spivs (2004)
programme.desc: A professional con-man spends his time honing the skills of his protege and making sure his gang of conniving criminals stays out of trouble. However, when a simple heist backfires the ragtag group are left with a lorry-load of illegal immigrants on their hands - and an angry smuggler with revenge on his mind soon adds to their woes. Crime drama, starring Ken Stott,, Nick Moran and Dominic Monaghan.(n)

programme.title: Mad Dog Morgan (1976)
programme.desc: An Irishman seeking his fortune in the Australian Gold Rush ends up in prison when driven to robbery out of desperation. After 12 years of abuse, he is released and becomes an outlaw, determined to make society pay for his suffering - and becoming a hero to the downtrodden in the process. Fact-based Western, starring Dennis Hopper and Jack Thompson.(n)

programme.title: The Repo Man
programme.desc: In recession-hit Britain, many people have turned to finance companies to purchase items over a fixed period of time. This documentary follows the life and work of bailiff Sean James as he confronts debtors across the nation who fail to keep up their repayments.(n)

programme.desc: Dwayne falls in love with Kate after she saves his life - much to Dee Dee's annoyance.(n)

programme.desc: Halvar lets Faxe steer the Drakkar home, but he becomes obsessed with chasing a whale.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Halvar's grandfather is discovered frozen alive. Once he is released from the ice, he sets out to get revenge on a giant and steal his treasure.(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: McMeer eats all the mangos on the island, so Zigby and his friends set sail in search of more.(n)

programme.desc: Everyone thinks a Bigfoot has stolen Lawrence's special shell when it disappears.(n)

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: Jack and Tashi are sucked into the Book of Tales, where they encounter a mixed-up monster and a knight who has lost his sword and shield.(n)

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: The boys go to the mountains in search of ice, and encounter an imp who freezes all the villagers.(n)

programme.desc: British house-hunters Ric and Nita Halstead, who are seeking a dream home in Miami, receive expert advice before buying. Amanda Lamb shows them round an art deco house, a Mediterranean-style villa and a Florida ranch.(n)

programme.title: Can't Bully Me
programme.desc: Documentary following four youngsters suffering from the traumatic effects of bullying as they attend the Red Balloon Learner Centres in Cambridge and north-west London, which deal with children unable to attend school due to past experiences. The film charts their progress as they overcome fears and rebuild their confidence to return to education and mainstream life.(n)

programme.title: Opening Up the Soane
programme.desc: Documentary about the extensive refurbishment of Sir John Soane's Museum to restore the building to its original glory.(n)

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: The story of the havoc caused by the V1 doodlebugs and the V2 rockets, and of the thousands of deaths that undermined the morale of a war-weary civilian population.(n)

programme.title: Mantovani TV Specials
programme.desc: Mantovani's 1950s TV series featuring special guests and hits of the time played by Mantovani and his Orchestra.(n)

programme.title: Malachi's Cove (1973)
programme.desc: A 14-year-old girl in a 19th-century Cornish village is left to fend for herself after her parents die. She finds a way to support herself and her wheelchair-bound grandfather by gathering seaweed along the coast and selling it. Family drama, starring Donald Pleasence, Veronica Quilligan, Peter Vaughan and Arthur English.(n)

programme.title: The Boy Who Stole a Million (1960)
programme.desc: A boy robs a bank to pay for repairs on his father's car, and is pursued by the police and criminals after the money. Comedy adventure, starring Virgilio Teixeira and Maurice Reyna.(n)

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White treks up The Great Orme in North Wales and beside a railway in West Yorkshire.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Amanda Lamb comes to the rescue of Peter Sullivan and Sally Turner, who are searching for an affordable holiday home on the Caribbean island of Barbados. Among the choices are a new apartment for £66,000 and a traditional villa.(n)

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: A bomb is found in a nightclub which German scientists claim cannot be disarmed, and Ash receives a mysterious message. Guest starring Judy Geeson (the original 1970s Poldark).(n)

programme.title: Filthy Cities
programme.desc: Dan Snow heads for 18th-century Paris to discover how the insanitary living conditions endured by its poorest residents helped bring about the French Revolution. He visits a perfumer to re-create some of the city's unsavoury smells, and finds out what happened to the thousands of bodies that overflowed from cemeteries during the Terror of 1793. He also tries his hand at tanning leather the traditional way - using dog faeces and urine.(n)

programme.title: Nuremberg - Nazis on Trial
programme.desc: Series recreating the court cases of three of the Second World War's most notorious Nazis, using eyewitness interviews and archive footage, as well as considering the interviews they underwent at the hands of lawyers and psychologists. The first episode focuses on the trial of Albert Speer, Hitler's chief architect and armaments minister, regarded by many as a 'good' Nazi. Nathaniel Parker stars.(n)

The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. programme.title: Strike It Lucky
programme.desc: Michael Barrymore hosts the madcap game show, in which three teams of two prove seek to progress along a board game-style path by answering questions. Each duo are allowed to determine how many spaces they would like to move by answering the corresponding number of questions. Landing on a space reveals it's hidden contents, which could amount to a prize, or a 'hot spot', which causes contestants to forfeit their gathered winnings.(n)
